linkedin_org_description:
ICAP CRIF (previously ICAP S.A. group of companies), with over 60 years of successful presence in Greece, Romania, Bulgaria & Cyprus, is the market leader in Credit Risk & Bus. Information solutions in SEE.
As of Dec. 23rd, 2021, is a wholly owned company of CRIF. It;s an important regional hub within CRIF Group and is organized & operates as a single entity under one common Management Team. Ιt provides innovative solutions to a wide range of business challenges,: Business Information & Credit Risk Solutions, Credit Rating Agency Services, Mktg. & Sales Solutions, Technology Solutions & Advanced Analytics and Consulting Services.
CRIF is a global company specializing in credit bureau and business information systems, analytics, outsourcing and processing services, as well as advanced digital and open banking solutions. Founded in Bologna (Italy) in 1988, today CRIF has an international presence, covering 37 countries across 4 continents with over 6,600 professionals.
CRIF aims to create value for consumers, businesses and financial institutions by providing information and solutions that enable more powerful decision-making, improve access to credit and accelerate digital innovation. CRIF also offers fraud prevention and cybersecurity services to consumers and SMEs.

Thanks to its two ESMA-authorized Credit Rating Agencies - both recognized as External Credit Assessment Institutions (ECAI) - CRIF provides regulatory ratings valid in all the European Union. Furthermore, CRIF operates as an Account Information Service Provider (AISP) in all European countries where the PSD2 directive applies with open banking services - including AISP services in the UK – and as an Account Aggregator (AA) in India.
- 37 countries covered
- 85+ subsidiary companies
- 6,600+ professionals at your service
- more than 10,500 financial institutions, 450 insurance companies, 90,000 business clients, and 1,000,000 consumers use CRIF services.
- 500+ software installations
http://www.crif.com

title: 022026_Αγγελία Μηχανικού Συντήρησης ref.022026
locations_alt_raw: Aspropyrgos, West Attica Regional Unit, Greece
description_text:
Η Vitex, είναι μία αμιγώς ελληνική βιομηχανία οικοδομικών χρωμάτων, η μεγαλύτερη στην Ελλάδα, με παρουσία από το 1960 και δίκτυο θυγατρικών εταιρειών και συνεργατών σε 10 ακόμη χώρες. Τα προϊόντα παράγονται αποκλειστικά και μόνο στο υπερσύγχρονο εργοστάσιο του Ασπροπύργου με κορυφαίες προδιαγραφές για την ποιότητα, την υγιεινή & ασφάλεια και τον σεβασμό προς το περιβάλλον και διακινούνται διαμέσου χιλιάδων εμπορικών συνεργατών. Η Vitex ζητά να προσλάβει:
Μηχανικός Συντήρησης- (ref. 022026) 
Με έδρα τον Ασπρόπυργο
Κύριες Αρμοδιότητες
Διενέργεια προληπτικής και διορθωτικής συντήρησης στα συστήματα παραγωγής, συσκευασίας και κτιριακών εγκαταστάσεων του εργοστασίου.
Καταγραφή βλαβών και επίλυση αυτών.
Έλεγχος ομαλής λειτουργίας συσκευαστικών μηχανών και παραγωγικού εξοπλισμού.
Δυνατότητα απασχόλησης σε κυλιόμενες βάρδιες- Πρωινή – Απογευματινή
Απαραίτητα προσόντα:
Πτυχίο ΙΕΚ ή ΕΠΑΛ Μηχανολογίας
Ελάχιστη εμπειρία 10 ετών σε βιομηχανικό περιβάλλον. Επιθυμητή η προϋπηρεσία σε χημική βιομηχανία
Καλές γνώσεις υδραυλικών, πνευματικών συστημάτων, πνευματικών αντλιών.
Καλές γνώσεις άρμοσης, εξάρμοσης μηχανολογικών εξαρτημάτων.
Καλές γνώσεις αντλιοστασίων και δεξαμενοστασίων
Γνώσεις Κατασκευαστικές και τεχνικές ικανότητες
Γνώσεις συντήρησης συσκευαστικών μηχανών, κυρίως γεμιστικών.
Επιθυμητή γνώση αγγλικής γλώσσας
Ομαδικότητα, υπευθυνότητα και συνέπεια
Εκπληρωμένες στρατιωτικές υποχρεώσεις
Η εταιρεία προσφέρει:
Ικανοποιητικές αποδοχές και άριστο εργασιακό περιβάλλον.
Μεγάλες προοπτικές εξέλιξης σε μια ταχύτατα αναπτυσσόμενη εταιρεία με ηγετική θέση στην ελληνική αγορά και τα Βαλκάνια.
Συνεχή εκπαίδευση και ανάπτυξη δεξιοτήτων.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Maintenance Engineer will be responsible for performing preventive and corrective maintenance on the factory's production, packaging, and building systems. This includes recording malfunctions, resolving issues, and ensuring the smooth operation of packaging machinery and production equipment.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a degree from IEK or EPAL in Mechanical Engineering and have a minimum of 10 years of experience in an industrial environment, with prior experience in the chemical industry being desirable. Essential technical knowledge includes hydraulics, pneumatics, assembly/disassembly, pumping/tank stations, and packaging machine maintenance.
linkedin_org_slogan: We commit to provide our customers with the most competitive total offering of products and services.
linkedin_org_specialties: Decorative - Architectural Coatings and Water proofing asphalt membranes
linkedin_org_locations: P.O.139 Imeros Topos, Aspropyrgos, Athens, 19300, GR
Kralja Petra I 89, N. Pazova, Belgrade, 22330, RS
Mirovyane village 1289, Stopanski dvor, Sofia, District Sofia City, BG
linkedin_org_description:
Established in 1932, VITEX S.A is the largest Greek owned company of architectural paints. The company has strong presence in South East Europe and affiliates in Serbia & Bulgaria. We provide high added value products to our customers with a consumer oriented approach that promotes sustainability, innovation, public health and environmental safety. We set trends in the markets we operate and have a strong belief that paint is not only a “sustainability catalyst” but also the finishing touch that will alter the space where we live, work, relax and enjoy our life. 
During the past decade a substantial investment in modernizing the production included the establishment of a state-of-the-art production plant, the largest paint factory in Greece and one of the most advanced in Europe. 
The Vitex brand is very well known in the professional and DIY markets. Our products are currently available in over 20 countries worldwide. 
VITEX S.A. is a family owned and managed, completely independent company with its own brands and know-how. Our sales exceed €40 million and we employ over 215 persons in sales, production, exports, marketing, quality control, product safety and R&D. 
The group consists today of four vertical business units, VITEX in architectural paints production and sales, HERMES in waterproofing materials/chemicals, VITEXTHERM in external thermal insulation composite systems (ETICS) and EUMARIA in yacht paints. The company has been certified as a Great Place to Work for the period 2020-2021.
Stay tuned as we will be sharing our news from this page!

title: Spa Therapist | Empiria Group (Season 2026)
description_text:
Spa Therapist
About us
Empiria Group owns, manages, and operates a distinctive collection of luxury hotels and villas, members of The Luxury Collection and Design Hotels™ by Marriott International, located in some of Greece’s most iconic destinations: Santorini, Paros, Milos and the Peloponnese.
With more than 30 years of expertise and a team of over 400 associates, Empiria Group is driven by a shared purpose: to create space for joy through elevated hospitality experiences. Our culture is shaped by four core values — finding joy, always elevating, exuding passion, and leading with purpose — guiding everything we do.
At Empiria Group, hospitality is more than a profession. It is a craft, a mindset, and a journey of continuous growth.
Role Overview
The Spa Therapist is responsible for providing high-quality spa treatments and wellness services to guests, ensuring that each guest receives a relaxing and personalized experience. This role requires a thorough understanding of spa treatments, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to create a calm, welcoming atmosphere for clients.
Key Accountabilities
Deliver high-quality spa treatments, including massages, facials, body treatments, and other specialized services, in line with spa standards and guests’ individual needs.
Conduct pre-treatment consultations to assess guests’ wellness needs and recommend appropriate treatments or spa packages.
Ensure an exceptional guest experience by clearly explaining treatment options, addressing questions, and creating a calm, welcoming, and relaxing environment.
Maintain the highest standards of cleanliness, hygiene, and safety in treatment rooms and spa areas, in accordance with company policies and health regulations.
Use, handle, and maintain spa equipment, products, and supplies responsibly, ensuring they are in excellent condition at all times.
Promote spa services and retail products, providing professional aftercare advice and recommending suitable products for home use when appropriate.
Maintain a professional appearance and demeanor at all times, acting as an ambassador of the spa’s values and philosophy.
Handle guest feedback and concerns with discretion and professionalism, ensuring timely and effective resolution of any issues.
Stay up to date with industry trends, new treatments, and product knowledge to continuously enhance service quality.
Support the daily operations of the spa, including preparation of treatment areas, linen management, and restocking of products and consumables.
Proven professional experience as a Spa Therapist, Massage Therapist, or in a similar role within a luxury hotel, resort, or high-end wellness environment.
Recognized certification or diploma in massage therapy and/or specialized spa treatments (e.g. facials, body therapies, holistic treatments).
Excellent command of the English language; knowledge of additional foreign languages will be considered a strong asset.
Candidate’s Profile
Professional, calm, and service-oriented demeanor.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to high standards.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Empathetic approach with a natural ability to make guests feel relaxed and comfortable.
Well-groomed appearance aligned with luxury spa standards.
Physically fit and able to perform treatments with consistency and precision.
At Empiria Group, our hotels are more than workplaces — they are environments where people grow, evolve, and find purpose.
Staff accommodation with Wi-Fi and air conditioning.
Half-board meal plan (Breakfast & Lunch).
Continuous training and development opportunities.
Career growth opportunities within Empiria Group and across Marriott International properties worldwide.
At Empiria Group we are proud to foster Equal Employment Opportunities regardless of race, color, ethnicity, religion, gender, age, disability, or sexual orientation. All applications are considered strictly confidential. After careful consideration of all the resumes received, we will only contact the candidates who meet the requirements of the job to arrange an interview. ​
You provide the Talent!
We provide the Space for Opportunity and Growth!
ai_benefits: Staff accommodation with Wi-Fi and air conditioning
Career growth opportunities within Empiria Group and across Marriott International properties worldwide
ai_core_responsibilities: The Spa Therapist is responsible for delivering high-quality spa treatments, such as massages and facials, tailored to guests' individual needs while ensuring a relaxing and personalized experience. Key duties include conducting pre-treatment consultations, maintaining impeccable cleanliness and safety standards in treatment areas, and promoting spa services and retail products.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have proven professional experience as a Spa Therapist, ideally within a luxury setting, and hold a recognized certification or diploma in relevant spa treatments. Excellent command of the English language is required, along with a professional, calm, service-oriented demeanor and strong interpersonal skills.
linkedin_org_slogan: Τhe Greek essence of distinctive hospitality
linkedin_org_locations: 22 Laodikis, Glyfada, Attiki 166 74, GR
Megalochori, Santorini, Cycladic Islands 84700, GR
Oia, Santorini, Cycladic Islands 84702, GR
Perivolos, Santorini, Cycladic Islands 84703, GR
Naousa, Kolympithres, Paros, Cycladic Islands 84401, GR
linkedin_org_description:
Empiria Group  is a privately owned Greek company created in 1989 by Antonis Eliopoulos. Today, Antonis Eliopoulos with his wife Kalia Konstantinidou own and manage successfully 3 well known and awarded 5* hotels and 6 Luxurious Villas in Santorini and two 5* hotels in Paros. Empiria Group elevates authentic, Greek hospitality through its distinct brands of organic, understated luxury. With a strong sense of location in the most exclusive destinations, each unique property offers a celebration of Greek escape for the experiential traveler by the most caring and positive associates.
Vedema Resort and Mystique Hotel located in Santorini are proud members of the Luxury Collection brand of Marriott International Inc
Istoria Hotel located in Santorini and Parilio Hotel located in Paros are proud members of Design Hotels of Marriott International Inc.

Deliver high-quality spa treatments, including massages, facials, body treatments, and other specialized services, in line with spa standards and guests’ individual needs.
Conduct pre-treatment consultations to assess guests’ wellness needs and recommend appropriate treatments or spa packages.
Ensure an exceptional guest experience by clearly explaining treatment options, addressing questions, and creating a calm, welcoming, and relaxing environment.
Maintain the highest standards of cleanliness, hygiene, and safety in treatment rooms and spa areas, in accordance with company policies and health regulations.
Use, handle, and maintain spa equipment, products, and supplies responsibly, ensuring they are in excellent condition at all times.
Promote spa services and retail products, providing professional aftercare advice and recommending suitable products for home use when appropriate.
Maintain a professional appearance and demeanor at all times, acting as an ambassador of the spa’s values and philosophy.
Handle guest feedback and concerns with discretion and professionalism, ensuring timely and effective resolution of any issues.
Stay up to date with industry trends, new treatments, and product knowledge to continuously enhance service quality.
Support the daily operations of the spa, including preparation of treatment areas, linen management, and restocking of products and consumables.
Proven professional experience as a Spa Therapist, Massage Therapist, or in a similar role within a luxury hotel, resort, or high-end wellness environment.
Recognized certification or diploma in massage therapy and/or specialized spa treatments (e.g. facials, body therapies, holistic treatments).
Excellent command of the English language; knowledge of additional foreign languages will be considered a strong asset.
Candidate’s Profile
Professional, calm, and service-oriented demeanor.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to high standards.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Empathetic approach with a natural ability to make guests feel relaxed and comfortable.
Well-groomed appearance aligned with luxury spa standards.
Physically fit and able to perform treatments with consistency and precision.
At Empiria Group, our hotels are more than workplaces — they are environments where people grow, evolve, and find purpose.
Staff accommodation with Wi-Fi and air conditioning.
Half-board meal plan (Breakfast & Lunch).
Continuous training and development opportunities.
Career growth opportunities within Empiria Group and across Marriott International properties worldwide.
At Empiria Group we are proud to foster Equal Employment Opportunities regardless of race, color, ethnicity, religion, gender, age, disability, or sexual orientation. All applications are considered strictly confidential. After careful consideration of all the resumes received, we will only contact the candidates who meet the requirements of the job to arrange an interview. ​
You provide the Talent!
We provide the Space for Opportunity and Growth!

title: SVP, Capital Markets & Structured Funding (Group), Fintech
description_text:
Optasia is a profitable, fast-growing fintech listed on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange (JSE). We operate across ~40 markets, partnering with leading mobile network operators and financial institutions. Our next phase of growth needs a senior capital markets leader who can design and execute the funding strategy that will power the platform over the next 3–5 years.
This is a true hands-on SVP role: you will sit with banks and institutional capital providers, shape structures, negotiate terms, and close transactions. If you like complex, real-world problems and you drive outcomes, you’ll have serious scope here.
Our culture
We’re a high-performance, low-ego team. People here take ownership, move fast, and say things clearly. We debate hard, align quickly, and then execute—no politics, no theatre. You’ll work with senior leaders who are hands-on, commercially sharp, and obsessed with delivery. We value trust, curiosity, and good judgment. If you like being accountable, solving real problems, and building something that matters at scale, you’ll feel at home.
You will own our future funding stack across:
Banks: bilateral, club, syndicated, RCF/term and structured lines
Structured funding: warehouse facilities, SPVs, receivables/contract-backed financing (where feasible)
Institutional credit: private credit, credit funds, debt hedge funds, special situations / debt PE
Capital markets: bond issuance (local/hard currency as appropriate), private placements
Hedging overlay: FX/IR approach aligned to cashflows and market constraints
You will build the group-level playbook and senior relationships that help our commercial teams execute locally—without losing control of risk, governance, and reporting discipline expected of a listed company.

What you’ll do
Build the 3–5 year funding strategy and convert it into an executable roadmap (base/upside/downside).
Lead end-to-end execution: origination → structuring → negotiation → documentation → close → lifecycle (reporting, covenants, waivers, refinancings).
Create repeatable structures and standards: security packages, waterfalls, covenant principles, reporting packs, data room and diligence readiness.
Establish and run our bond issuance capability (when appropriate): process, materials, counterparties, and ongoing obligations.
Build senior relationships across banks and institutional capital, and maintain a sharp view of market terms and pricing.
Partner closely with Risk, Legal, Tax, Treasury and Revenue teams to deliver outcomes with clean governance and a strong audit trail.

What you will bring

You’re independent, outcomes-focused, and comfortable owning hard decisions. You can go from strategy to signature without drama.
15+ years in capital markets / structured finance / leveraged finance / private credit (emerging markets experience strongly valued).
Proven track record leading complex debt transactions end-to-end with banks and institutional credit providers.
Bond issuance experience (lead role preferred): local and/or hard currency; private placement and/or Reg S/144A where relevant.
Strong structuring judgment: security, covenants, waterfalls, intercompany mechanics; plus strong modelling and stress testing.
Credibility in front of senior counterparties—able to negotiate with credit committees and investment committees.
A highly experienced Group Treasurer / Corporate Treasurer who has personally led structured funding programs (warehouse/SPV/securitisation-style, complex syndicated/structured facilities, private credit raises, and/or capital markets issuance). This is not an operational cash-management profile.
Flexibility with frequent travel (Africa + key financial centers)

Why you should apply
A senior, visible mandate with direct access to the Group CFO and real influence on the company’s growth path.
The chance to build a funding platform that works across multiple jurisdictions and real constraints—complex, but rewarding.
A profitable business with momentum, operating at scale and held to public-company standards.
A collaborative, open culture where ownership and delivery are recognised.

What we offer: 

💸 Competitive remuneration package 
🏝 Extra day off on your birthday 
💰 Performance-based bonus scheme 
👩🏽‍⚕️ Comprehensive private healthcare insurance 
📲 💻 All the tech gear you need to work smart 

Optasia’s Perks: 

🎌 Be a part of a multicultural working environment 
🎯 Meet a very unique and promising business and industry 
🌌 🌠 Gain insights for tomorrow market’s foreground 
🎓 A solid career path within our working family is ready for you 
📚 Continuous training and access to online training platforms 
🥳 CSR activities and festive events within any possible occasion
Optasia’s Values 🌟 

#1 Drive to Thrive: Fully dedicated to evolving. We welcome all challenges and learning opportunities. 
#2 Customer-First Mindset: We go above and beyond to meet our partners’ and clients’ expectations. 
#3 Bridge the Gap: Knowledge is shared, information is exchanged and every opinion counts. 
#4 Go-Getter Spirit: We are results oriented. We identify any shortcomings that hold us back and step up to do what’s needed. 
#5 Together we will do it: We are committed to supporting one another and to understanding and respecting different perspectives, as we aim to reach our common goals.
ai_benefits: Extra day off on your birthday
All the tech gear you need to work smart
ai_core_responsibilities: The SVP will design and execute a 3-5 year funding strategy, converting it into an executable roadmap, and leading the end-to-end execution of all debt transactions from origination through lifecycle management. This role also involves creating repeatable structures, establishing bond issuance capability, and building senior relationships with capital providers.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have 15+ years of experience in capital markets, structured finance, leveraged finance, or private credit, with a proven track record leading complex debt transactions end-to-end. Essential qualifications include strong structuring judgment, credibility with senior counterparties, and experience leading structured funding programs or capital markets issuance.
linkedin_org_locations: JLT, Dubai, AE
Kifisias 16 & Chalepa, Marousi, Athens 15121, GR
linkedin_org_description:
We deliver Optasia, the AI Platform enabling financial access for the next billion customers. We are changing the world our way.

Α versatile, data-driven B2B2X (business-to-business-to-customers and SMEs) financial technology platform for MNOs, mobile wallet operators and financial institutions, which provides them with accurate credit scoring for responsible financing decision-making. 

We support intelligent credit decisions, enable a broad range of financial services development and drive successful business models. All with ease, through our AI-led fully-automated decisioning algorithm, which allows us to efficiently make millions of decisions on financing, based on sophisticated data analysis.

Thanks to our technology and network we provide powerful flywheel effects with repeatable and scalable growth levers, to help our partners grow and enable financial access to retail & SME customers across numerous markets.

Operating since 2012 and expanded in 38 countries (as of October 2025) in Africa, Middle East, Asia and Europe with focus in emerging markets, Optasia is enabling credit decisions for an average of over 120 million customers a month and had more than 860 million addressable subscribers in 2024, continuing to expand to new markets globally.

Optasia has been chosen as an investment opportunity by top firms such as Abu Dhabi's Waha Capital in 2017, and Ethos, a leading South African investment company in late 2018. The company also obtained ISO 27001 Certification in late 2021. Founded as Channel VAS in 2012, we became Optasia in 2022, incorporating a brand identity that more broadly reflects our capabilities.

title: Freelance Automotive Engineer with Python Experience - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design graduate- and industry-level automotive engineering problems grounded in real practice; 
Evaluate AI-generated solutions for correctness, assumptions, and engineering logic; 
Validate analytical or numerical results using Python (NumPy, SciPy, Pandas); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with first principles and accepted engineering standards; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to assess multi-step problem solving. 
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for automotive engineers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Automotive Engineering or related fields, e.g. Mechatronics, Manufacturing Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, etc. 
3+ years of professional automotive engineering experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PE, CEng, PMP) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $40/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.
ai_core_responsibilities: Contributors will design automotive engineering problems and evaluate AI-generated solutions for correctness. They will also validate results using Python and improve AI reasoning to align with engineering standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a degree in Automotive Engineering or related fields and at least 3 years of professional experience. Strong proficiency in Python and English is required, with additional certifications being advantageous.
linkedin_org_slogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
linkedin_org_description:
Welcome to Mindrift — a space where innovation meets opportunity. We’re a pioneering platform dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence through collaborative online projects. Our focus lies in creating data for generative AI, offering a unique chance for freelancers to contribute to AI development from anywhere, at any time.

At Mindrift, we believe in the power of collective intelligence to shape the future of AI. Our platform allows users to dive into a variety of tasks — ranging from creating training prompts for AI models to refining AI responses for greater relevance.

Let’s build the future of AI together, one task at a time.

title: Skandi Jupiter Chief Engineer 02.07.2026
description_text:
Join the Skandi Jupiter team as a Chief Engineer (for internal applicants only)
DOF is excited to announce opportunities on our prestigious vessel, Skandi Jupiter, as we continue to uphold our commitment to excellence in offshore services. We are seeking dedicated team members to join us on this exciting journey starting from July 2nd 2026. Handover period prior to embarking is expected.
Curent rotation - 4 weeks.
As part of the crew on Skandi Jupiter, you will be an integral part of our operations, contributing to various tasks and ensuring that we maintain the highest standards of safety and efficiency in our work.
We value our employees and strive to create an environment that fosters teamwork, innovation, and professional growth. If you are looking to advance your career in the offshore industry and be part of a leading organization, DOF is the place for you!
Who we are 
DOF is a leading provider of integrated marine and subsea services to the global offshore energy market. We combine expertise, assets, and technology to execute integrated subsea and offshore operations safely, and reliably - every time. 
With a multinational team of approximately 6,000 people and a fleet of modern offshore/subsea vessels, we support our partners with global reach, scale, and industry-leading capability. Our operational centres and business units are in Norway, Denmark, UK, USA, Singapore, Brazil, Argentina, Canada, Angola, Philippines, and Australia. 
At DOF you join a trusted global organisation where safety, teamwork, and integrity come first. We offer strong career growth, supportive development, and a culture that values inclusion, recognition, and connection. Build a meaningful career in a workplace that puts people first. 
Unlocking the future, together. 
Certifications and qualifications as per the position applied for on Skandi Jupiter.
Proven experience within the offshore industry, particularly in roles related to vessel operations.
Strong commitment to safety and adherence to regulations.
Ability to work in a team-based environment and contribute positively to crew dynamics.
Flexible with working hours and conditions typical of offshore environments.
Excellent communication skills in English; proficiency in other languages is a plus.
Tags: STCW certification, Huet, Ahts experience 
Please note: Actual positions and responsibilities will be confirmed closer to the start date.
At DOF, we believe in providing our employees with a supportive and enriching work environment. Here are some of the benefits you can enjoy as part of our team: 
Worldwide Fleet: Be a part of our global operations and gain exposure to a diverse range of projects and locations. 
Career Growth: We encourage internal mobility and provide opportunities to grow within the company, whether it's moving upwards or exploring new roles sideways. 
Wages based on Norwegain CBA

title: Account Manager - Regional
description_text:
JOB PURPOSE:
The Account Manager is responsible for managing client relationships, leading day-to-day operations, and ensuring the successful delivery of campaigns. Acting as the main point of contact for clients, the Account Manager balances strategic thinking with flawless project execution. This role might mentor junior team members and collaborate closely with internal departments to maintain high-quality standards, client satisfaction, and account profitability. 
DIMENSIONS:
Financial: Responsible for input and monitoring of financial estimates, budgets, and profitability tracking for assigned accounts.
Non-Financial: Responsible for client servicing quality, internal coordination, and team development within assigned projects.
RESPONSIBILITIES AND TASKS:
Act as the focal point for assigned client accounts, leading day-to-day communication and ensuring alignment with client expectations.
Manage projects from briefing to delivery, including timelines, budgets, production, and reporting ensuring on-time and high-quality execution.
Develop clear and insightful client briefs that translate marketing objectives into actionable plans for internal teams.
Lead the preparation and delivery of client presentations, ensuring strategic and creative clarity.
Oversee social and digital content delivery, ensuring all materials adhere to approved calendars and brand guidelines.
Build and maintain strong client relationships based on trust, accountability, and value creation.
Identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities in collaboration with senior management.
Partner with strategy, creative, and media teams to deliver integrated campaigns and proactive recommendations.
Provide strategic input in discussions related to market trends, audience insights, and competitive landscapes.
Support quality assurance across all deliverables, reviewing materials before client submission.
Mentor Account Executives and Senior Account Executives, providing guidance, feedback, and development opportunities.
Handle conflict resolution and problem-solving within accounts to ensure smooth collaboration and client satisfaction.
Report on account financials as per agency processes.
WORK CONTACTS:
Internal: Account Management, Strategy, Creative, Studio, Media, and Finance Teams.
External: Clients, Production Houses, Suppliers, and External Partners.
JOB SPECIFICATIONS:
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Advertising, Business, or a related field.
Experience: 4–6 years of experience in account management or client servicing, including managing medium-to-large accounts within an advertising or marketing agency.
SKILLS
Quality and Delivery
Ability to review and QA all deliverables for accuracy and brand alignment.
Ability to maintain attention to detail under pressure and tight timelines.
Ability to enforce process discipline across briefing, revisions, and approvals.
Client and Strategic Relationships
Ability to act as the main focal point for clients and manage expectations.
Ability to build trust through reliability, responsiveness, and value creation.
Ability to spot upsell and cross-sell opportunities with senior guidance.
Strategic and Analytical
Ability to translate business and marketing objectives into clear briefs.
Ability to connect audience, market, and competitive insights to recommendations.
Ability to align execution with the client’s strategy and KPIs.
Communication and Presentation
Ability to lead external presentations with clarity and structure.
Ability to write concise briefs, contact reports, status updates, and proposals.
Ability to handle difficult conversations with diplomacy and professionalism.
Project and Financial Management
Ability to plan and manage timelines, scopes, resources, and dependencies.
Ability to build estimates and track budgets, billing, and profitability.
Ability to maintain accurate documentation and forecasting per agency processes.
Leadership and Team Development
Ability to coach AEs and SAEs, delegate tasks, and review outputs.
Ability to drive collaboration across strategy, creative, media, studio, and production.
Ability to resolve issues and remove blockers to keep work moving.
Personal Attributes
Strong ownership mindset and accountability for outcomes.
High organization and prioritization in a fast-paced environment.
Proactive problem solving with a solution-first approach.
Digital and Social Execution
Ability to oversee accurate and timely social posting against approved calendars.
Ability to enforce platform best practices and brand guidelines.
Languages and Tools
Fluency in English and Arabic, spoken and written.
Proficiency with presentation, spreadsheet, and project management tools.

This role involves balancing strategic thinking with flawless project execution while monitoring account profitability.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates need a Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Advertising, Business, or a related field, coupled with 4–6 years of experience in account management within an agency setting, specifically managing medium-to-large accounts. Fluency in both English and Arabic is also required.

title: Design Engineer
description_text:
Winthrop Technologies is Europe’s only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. 
We are currently seeking a Design Engineer to join our growing design team. Reporting to the Design Manager, the Design Engineer will support the development, coordination, and delivery of high-quality design solutions on a major data centre project in Finland. 
Key Responsibilities
Support the Design Manager in the development and coordination of project designs from concept through to construction and commissioning. 
Produce, review, and coordinate design drawings, calculations, and specifications in line with project requirements and relevant standards. 
Liaise with internal design teams, BIM teams, and external consultants to ensure coordinated and clash-free designs. 
Assist with the Technical Submittal (TS) process, ensuring submissions align with project specifications and programme requirements. 
Respond to design queries and Requests for Information (RFIs) in a timely and accurate manner. 
Support the preparation and review of documentation and revisions. 
Participate in design coordination meetings, workshops, and internal reviews. 
Support change management processes by identifying design changes and assessing impacts on cost, programme, and constructability. 
Ensure design deliverables comply with quality, health & safety, and environmental requirements. 
Assist with the preparation of as-built drawings, O&M documentation, and final handover requirements.
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction, or a related discipline. 
2–5 years’ experience in a design engineering role within construction, engineering consultancy, or contracting environments. 
Experience on large-scale projects such as data centres, industrial, pharmaceutical, or commercial developments is preferred. 
Strong understanding of design coordination and construction methodologies. 
Experience working with BIM and digital construction tools is desirable. 
Proficiency in MS Office applications; experience with AutoCAD, Revit, or similar design software is advantageous. 
Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. 
Excellent communication and teamwork skills. 
A proactive, detail-oriented, and solution-focused approach.
Work with a market leader in the Data centre Construction industry 
Be part of a forward-thinking team delivering critical infrastructure in a high-growth sector 
Long-term career progression opportunities and professional development support. 
Competitive salary packages 
Winthrop Technologies Ltd. are an equal opportunities employer
ai_core_responsibilities: The Design Engineer will support the Design Manager in developing and coordinating project designs from concept through construction and commissioning, while producing, reviewing, and coordinating necessary technical documentation like drawings and specifications. Key tasks include liaising with internal teams and external consultants to ensure coordinated designs and assisting with technical submittals and responding to design queries.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction, or a related field, coupled with 2–5 years of design engineering experience in construction or consultancy environments. Strong organizational skills, excellent communication, and proficiency with design software like AutoCAD or Revit are highly valued.

Winthrop Technologies is a dedicated data centre delivery partner providing turnkey solutions to our clients, headquartered in Dublin and delivering throughout Europe. 

Winthrop specialises in providing turnkey data centre solutions, providing services from design, through to construction across the full range of civil, structural, architectural, mechanical and electrical services as well as commissioning. 

Winthrop has grown to become the leading and most trusted company in our sector and is the only dedicated Data Centre delivery partner in Europe.

We are currently constructing multiple turnkey data centre projects in 7 different European countries, equating to over 430 MW of IT load in flight.

title: Senior .NET Developer
description_text:
PRODYNA focuses on customer experience, by designing, implementing, and operating custom software applications for mid to large size enterprises. We are committed to offering our customers innovative and future-proof solutions, through digitalisation and cloud computing strategies. As a member of Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), PRODYNA promotes speed, agility, and scalability in software development.
We are looking for a Senior Software Developer to join our growing team and help design, build, and maintain scalable web applications.
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and maintain high-quality applications using .NET and Angular 
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver robust and scalable solutions 
Participate in architectural decisions and code reviews 
Ensure best practices in performance, security, and maintainability 
Contribute to continuous improvement of development processes 
Required Skills & Experience
Strong experience with .NET (C#) in a senior-level role 
Solid hands-on experience with Angular 
Experience building and consuming RESTful APIs 
Strong understanding of software design principles and patterns 
Ability to work independently and take ownership of features
Compensation & Perks
Salary: We will settle on the exact compensation amount based on prior experience and skills.
Private health insurance & Life Insurance from day #1
Health management scheme (weekly sessions &monthly challenges)
25 vacation days
Team events tech oriented and more
International network
Lunch in the office & Go For Eat vouchers 
Employee referral programme/ bonus
Dedicated budget for:
Employee education ~ 800€
Hardware selection (MacBook or Lenovo ThinkPad) with your own mobile ~ 3000€
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves designing, developing, and maintaining high-quality applications utilizing .NET and Angular, while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver robust and scalable solutions. Key duties also include participating in architectural decisions, conducting code reviews, and ensuring adherence to best practices in performance, security, and maintainability.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess strong senior-level experience with .NET (C#) and solid hands-on experience with Angular, including building and consuming RESTful APIs. A strong understanding of software design principles and patterns is required, along with the ability to work independently and take ownership of features.

title: Deployment & Maintenance Software Engineer
description_text:
Derq is an MIT spinoff building AI-powered traffic safety and smart infrastructure. We’re a team of passionate innovators, leveraging the latest in AI and technology to transform the future of mobility. Our platform enhances road safety and traffic management by turning real-time data into actionable insights for cities and road operators. Our patented technology collects and analyzes data from connected sensors like cameras, radar, and traffic signal controllers to help predict and prevent road incidents. We deploy edge and cloud solutions that make intersections and highways safer and smarter.
Role Overview
We are looking for a Deployment & Maintenance Software Engineer to support the setup, deployment, and ongoing reliability of our software and hardware across client projects.
In this role, you will manage server configurations, oversee deployments, monitor live systems, and help keep performance and uptime high. You will work closely with Project Management, Software Systems, and System/DevOps teams to troubleshoot issues, improve configurations, and continuously raise the quality of what we deliver after go-live.
Key Responsibilities
Configure and maintain Derq’s software for stable, optimal performance
Troubleshoot and resolve server issues quickly to minimize downtime
Plan and execute software releases, updates, and security patches
Ensure configurations are consistent, compliant, and repeatable across deployments
Deploy and set up software on new hardware and new sites efficiently
Monitor deployed systems and continuously improve performance and uptime
Identify process gaps and implement practical improvements to speed up deployments and reduce incidents
Maintain clear documentation for configurations, deployments, and system status
Collaborate with engineering teams to support smooth integrations and issue resolution
Troubleshoot deployment and network issues using standard tools (e.g., SSH, ping, traceroute)
Participate in occasional shifts to support global clients and maintain reliability
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (Computer Science, Information Technology, Electrical Engineering, or similar)
2-3 years of experience in server configuration, maintenance, and deployments
Hands-on experience in Linux/Ubuntu environments, including: 
Basic Bash scripting (e.g., creating folders, generating multiple files) 
Basic Python scripting for automation 
Solid understanding of networking fundamentals, including: 
SSH and secure connections (ports, authentication methods) 
Public vs private IP addresses 
TCP vs UDP 
Routing concepts (static vs dynamic) 
NAT, port forwarding, and port mapping 
Strong troubleshooting skills with a practical, step-by-step approach to resolving connectivity issues and server downtime
Familiarity with common diagnostic tools (e.g., ifconfig/ipconfig, ping, traceroute)
Strong communication skills and ability to work well with cross-functional teams
Willingness and ability to overlap 4–5 hours daily with global teams for collaboration, with on-call support if needed (stable power and reliable internet required for remote work)
High learning agility and comfort adapting to new tools, systems, and processes
Good to have
Understanding of hardware components and on-site infrastructure
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)
Familiarity with virtualization and containerization (e.g., VMware, Docker)
Exposure to configuration management tools (e.g., Ansible, Puppet, Chef)
ai_core_responsibilities: This role involves configuring and maintaining software for optimal performance, troubleshooting server issues quickly to minimize downtime, and executing software releases, updates, and security patches across client projects. The engineer will also monitor live systems and continuously improve performance and uptime after go-live.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a Bachelor's degree in a relevant field and 2-3 years of experience in server configuration, maintenance, and deployments, with hands-on experience in Linux/Ubuntu environments and basic scripting in Bash and Python. A solid understanding of networking fundamentals, strong troubleshooting skills, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Intelligent transportation systems for smarter, safer roads
linkedin_org_locations: Detroit, US
Dubai, AE
linkedin_org_description:
We are innovators and believers—driven by our mission to eliminate road fatalities through AI-powered intelligent transportation systems. As an MIT-spinoff, our academic roots in research drive our passion to advance smart cities, improve road safety, and solve transportation challenges.

Commercialized in 2016 from an idea to revolutionary AI, our ITS technology is live today in 30+ cities across the U.S. and GCC. As we’ve grown, our work has contributed to 15 patents, helped save countless lives, and earned global recognition from AI and mobility leaders—including the WEF, SXSW, Techstars, ITS America, Vision Zero, and Together for Safer Roads. 

To learn more about our AI traffic systems that detect, track, classify, and predict, visit Derq.com.

title: Principal Consultant
description_text:
Get to know PeopleSense by Altius
PeopleSense by Altius, a subsidiary of Altius Group, has been in operation since 1998. PeopleSense provide psychological services to a range of clients in business, government and privately, throughout Australia. As a market and innovation leader in the industry, our team of over 150+ psychologists across Australia, support people to realise their health potential by delivering contemporary and evidence based interventions to clients and customers.

Why you will love working with us
Be warmly welcomed into your new team with a 3-month Induction Program
Take control of your career development and progression with tailored career pathways (including clinical expertship, account management, or team management opportunities).
We prioritise professional development and offer annual allowances towards training based on individualised career goals (including Board Approved Supervisor Training, EMDR, Schema & Gottman’s training).
Engage with your upline Principal Consultant for weekly/ fortnightly peer supervision and coaching.
Our remuneration packages comprise of competitive base salaries, performance-based salary reviews, and wellbeing benefits.
We are proud to acknowledge 76% of our promotions in the last year were to female members of our team.
Our Wellbeing Promise
Experience genuine support for your personal wellbeing including access to our AltiusLife wellness app, regular wellness seminars, and Employee Assistance Program.
We value leave! We offer our team members paid parental leave plus purchased leave arrangements to optimise your work-life balance.
You will receive the latest technology for efficient working, including a Surface Pro and smartphones
We believe meaningful work is essential to job satisfaction and provides a sense of purpose. That's why we offer job diversification across multiple work streams, giving you the opportunity to work in a variety of settings and truly make a difference.
Your new role
This is a full time position based in Canberra ACTAs a Principal Psychologist at PeopleSense by Altius you will lead a team of Psychologists who deliver a range of clinical and organisational psychology focused services. Your role includes the following:
Team leadership
Oversee a small team of provisional, registered, and clinical psychologists.
Provide guidance, support & mentorship, assisting in performance management, career progression, and business operations.
Clinical expertship
Facilitate and participate in clinical supervision of client caseload as needed, ensuring the highest standard of care is delivered.
Undertake quality assurance activities to maintain and improve service delivery excellence.
Psychological service delivery
Provide psychological services to a caseload of clients and customers by delivering evidence-based interventions and therapeutic support, including:
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) counselling – offering services to a range of clients, including adults, children and adolescences and couples
Onsite and Critical Incident Attendance – support people at their workplace
Private / Medicare rebated counselling
Compensation Scheme Work – support our clients with pain related issues, injury adjustment and trauma from an acquired injury
Multidisciplinary Interventions – work in a multidisciplinary team to support client outcomes
Psychological Assessment 

About you
Psychologist with full registration with AHPRA (Australia Health Practitioner Regulation Agency) 
Experience leading a team and supporting psychologists is essential
Full Australian working rights
Ability to obtain a national police check
A current driver's licence and a comprehensively insured vehicle
Apply Now
If you would like to have a confidential conversation before applying or if you’re interested in a career with Altius, please email Natalia at ndmochowska@altius-group.com.au.

Altius is an Equal Opportunity Employer and an inclusive workforce. Research has shown that, in Australia, women, people living with disability, LGBTQIA+, First Nations people, and those from minority groups are less likely to apply for a role because they feel they will not be successful. We believe in the strength of diversity and inclusion, and we would like to strongly encourage those who identify with any of these groups to apply for a role with us.

You will also provide direct psychological services to a caseload covering EAP counselling, critical incident attendance, private/Medicare rebated counselling, and compensation scheme work.

Applicants must also hold full Australian working rights and be able to obtain a national police check, along with holding a current driver's license and insured vehicle.

linkedin_org_description:
WORKPLACE HEALTH & WELLBEING SOLUTIONS THAT AIM HIGHER

Work related injuries, psychosocial hazards and lifestyle driven health risks cost Australian employers billions every year in lost productivity, rising premiums and talent attrition. Altius meets this challenge with comprehensive, health and wellbeing solutions that cover physical, psychological and social needs— providing tailored support at every stage of the employee journey, driving safe, sustainable, high-performing workplaces that power business success. 

Altius integrates four specialist brands into one seamless ecosystem, guiding organisations from prevention to peak performance: 

*PeopleSense: Psychology & Wellbeing by Altius – Builds workforce resilience and engagement through psychological, physical and organisational support programmes, helping retention and employees thrive. 

*Executive Health Solutions by Altius – Gives your organisation a competitive edge by ensuring senior leaders operate at their peak via evidencebased assessments and targeted interventions that mitigate health risks. 

*Rehabilitation Services by Altius – Minimises downtime and claims costs by guiding injured employees safely back to work, rebuilding confidence and capacity through multidisciplinary occupational rehabilitation. 

*Workplace Health & Safety by Altius – Turns WHS from compliance into a strategic asset, embedding proactive prevention and integrated management of psychosocial and physical risks to protect your workforce. 

Call 1800 258 487 or email referrals@altius-group.com.au to find out how we can help.

title: Business Development Manager - Marketing
description_text:
JOB PURPOSE:
Sales and New Business Manager will drive agency growth by identifying and securing new business opportunities across various industries. This role leads outreach efforts, identifies & responds to RFPs and builds strong new client relationships. The ideal candidate will bring a strong commercial mindset, and a proven track record in revenue generation within the advertising sector.
2- RESPONSIBILITIES AND TASKS:
● Conduct outreach and identify qualified leads in line with the annual new business strategy, while leading on commercial proposals and required documentation.
● Achieve revenue targets by uncovering new market opportunities and presenting tailored service offerings in collaboration with the team.
● Regularly update the sales pipeline, report key insights, and refine strategies to align with business objectives.
● Monitor market trends and competitors to develop proactive, customized briefs based on client needs in Egypt and the broader GCC region (as needed).
● Build strong client relationships as a trusted advisor, engaging new clients and agency partners to deliver impactful solutions.
● Represent the company at industry events to enhance brand visibility and establish thought leadership.
3- WORK CONTACTS:
Internal Direct communication with Account Management, Creatives, Production, Media Buying, Consumer Insights ,and Finance
External Different clients
4- JOB SPECIFICATIONS:
Education
Bachelor’s degree in related field
Experience
Minimum of 4 years of experience in new business development within advertising or
marketing industry 
Skills
● Proven experience in sales, business development, or a related role, with a strong focus on lead generation and new business opportunities in digital and/or advertising.
● Demonstrated ability to identify and convert leads through insight-driven commercial proposals.
● Strong understanding of industry dynamics and emerging trends in Egypt and the broader GCC region.
● Track record of developing and executing effective sales strategies that drive measurable growth.
● Proficient in maintaining accurate and up-to-date records of sales activities and client interactions using CRM systems.
● Experience working collaboratively with cross-functional teams across departments in a fast-paced environment.
● Excellent networking, communication, and presentation skills, with a proven ability to build and maintain long-term client relationships.
Working Conditions
The work environment is fast-paced and deadline-driven
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales and New Business Manager is tasked with driving agency growth by identifying and securing new business opportunities across various industries through outreach and responding to RFPs. This role involves achieving revenue targets, updating the sales pipeline, and building strong, trusted advisor relationships with new clients and agency partners.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a minimum of four years of experience in new business development within the advertising or marketing industry, coupled with a strong commercial mindset and a proven track record in revenue generation. Essential skills include insight-driven commercial proposal development, understanding regional market dynamics (Egypt/GCC), and proficiency in using CRM systems.

title: Sales Manager (Polish Market) I TELEMATICS
description_text:
We are looking for a Sales Manager to work with Poland market and strengthen our ambitious sales team. You will work with the leading telematics solutions in the world and master your B2B sales skills.
Teltonika’s telematics business unit develops and produces easy-to-use GPS tracking devices for effective fleet management in logistics, agriculture, car sharing, construction, and more. Using the latest technologies, Teltonika sets new standards for the telematics industry.
Teltonika provides an opportunity for every ambitious sales representative to improve their sales skills, develop comprehensive knowledge of products, and generate considerable earnings.
In this role, you will:
Contribute to market research, including identify market potential and find opportunities for growth
Build and maintain long-term relationships with new and existing customers through daily contact by cold calling, making presentations
Prepare commercial offers and ensure contracts to achieve assigned sales goals
Attend exhibitions/meetings abroad to represent Teltonika and its products with solutions
Collaborate with technical support department and product specialists to address customer requirements
Leverage CRM systems to process client information
What we expect of you:
1+ year experience in B2B and/or B2C sales, with results that you are proud of
Excellent Polish and English language skills (both spoken and written), as it will be your primary working language
Organized and results-oriented approach, a challenge-driven personality
Ambition to generate strong earnings by conquering foreign markets with an innovative product from Lithuania
Willing to travel abroad
Familiar with CRM or data-driven tools
Driver's license
What we offer:
Great opportunities to grow in a strong and rapidly expanding company.
Interesting and ambitious projects of industrial GPS tracking devices.
Place where curiosity is key: here, we share knowledge and learn in training courses, conferences or business trips.
Dynamic, challenging work environment and cooperation-based internal culture.
Care about your emotional and physical well-being by offering flexible work schedules and health insurance.
Company-paid sports activities (basketball, football, volleyball, yoga) and other sports events.
Celebrating achievements and having fun together in team building, summer festivals, and other important occasions.
Opportunity for solid bonuses when participating in the recommendation program.
We delight with birthday and Christmas gifts and rewards on work anniversaries. 
Lunch snacks together at the dinner or lunch table
Salary
We appreciate everyone's efforts and experiences, so we offer an attractive salary matching your skillset and field expertise. The salary for this position is 12 Eur/h - 15 Eur/h (~2000 eur/month - 2500 eur/month) pre-tax + attractive bonus package (including monthly bonuses) based on your results and sales growth.
*In an employment agreement we specify and define a fixed (tariff) hourly salary rate before taxes.

If you have questions, do not hesitate to contact our recruitment colleague Aistė Mataitytė, email aiste.mataityte@teltonika.lt.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Manager will contribute to market research to identify growth opportunities and build/maintain long-term customer relationships through daily contact, including cold calling and presentations. They will also prepare commercial offers, ensure contracts are signed to meet sales goals, and represent the company at international events.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have over one year of proven B2B and/or B2C sales experience with measurable results, along with excellent proficiency in both Polish and English languages. The role requires an organized, results-oriented approach, ambition for high earnings, willingness to travel abroad, and familiarity with CRM tools.
linkedin_org_locations: K. Baršausko g. 66, Kaunas, Lithuania 51436, LT
linkedin_org_description: Teltonika Networks is a rapidly growing technology company, manufacturing professional network connectivity equipment for international markets. Through long-term experience and research and development of industrial network devices for IoT and M2M communication, we have developed a wide portfolio of products for the most complex areas of Industry 4.0, Smart City, and Green Energy.

title: IAM Professional Services
description_text:
About the Role
We’re looking for an IAM Operations Engineer with 3–5 years of experience to support and run SailPoint-based identity services in a production environment. This role is hands-on and operational, focused on keeping IAM services stable, secure, and running smoothly while continuously improving processes and automation.
Key Responsibilities
Support day-to-day operations of SailPoint IdentityIQ and/or IdentityNow
Handle identity lifecycle events (joiners, movers, leavers)
Monitor provisioning jobs, connectors, and integrations
Troubleshoot access issues, failed provisioning, and SailPoint errors
Support access requests, approvals, and certifications
Work with application teams to onboard and maintain integrations
Assist with SailPoint upgrades, patches, and changes
Maintain runbooks, SOPs, and operational documentation
Support audits and compliance activities when needed
Look for opportunities to improve automation and operational efficiency
Required Skills & Qualifications
3–5 years of experience in IAM or security operations
Hands-on experience with SailPoint (IdentityIQ or IdentityNow)
Solid understanding of identity lifecycle management
Experience with access provisioning and governance processes
Familiarity with Active Directory, Azure AD, and cloud apps
Comfortable working with ITSM tools (ServiceNow or similar)
Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
Preferred Qualifications
Basic scripting or automation (PowerShell, Java, REST APIs)
Experience in managed services or production IAM environments
Exposure to audits or compliance frameworks
SailPoint certification or formal training
ai_core_responsibilities: The IAM Operations Engineer will support the day-to-day operations of SailPoint identity services, handling identity lifecycle events and troubleshooting access issues. The role also involves maintaining operational documentation and looking for opportunities to improve automation and efficiency.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3-5 years of experience in IAM or security operations, with hands-on experience in SailPoint. A solid understanding of identity lifecycle management and familiarity with Active Directory and cloud applications is also required.

title: GTM Engineer
description_text:
About Huzzle
At Huzzle, we connect high-performing B2B sales professionals with global companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include startups, digital agencies, and tech platforms in industries like SaaS, MarTech, FinTech, and EdTech. We match top sales talent to full-time remote roles where they’re hired directly into client teams and provided ongoing support by Huzzle.
Role Type: Full-time
Engagement: Independent Contractor 
Hours: UK Timezone 
Job Summary
We’re looking for a GTM Engineer—a hybrid strategist and technologist who integrates sales, marketing, and product platforms to drive efficient and scalable revenue operations. If you love solving system bottlenecks, building automations, and working at the intersection of data and business, this role is for you.
Key Responsibilities
Connect CRM, marketing, and product systems to ensure unified data flow and operational efficiency 
Automate lead generation, enrichment, and outreach workflows to reduce manual tasks and increase productivity 
Build and manage data pipelines that support real-time visibility into sales and marketing performance 
Analyze engagement metrics and GTM workflows to identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements 
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business objectives into scalable technical solutions 
Continuously test, optimize, and refine GTM systems to support faster, smarter revenue operations
Experience with CRMs like Salesforce, HubSpot, and marketing automation tools. 
Proficiency in no-code/low-code platforms (Zapier, Make, Tray.io, Airtable, etc.). 
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret performance data and generate strategic insights. 
Experience building scalable GTM systems or revenue operations workflows is a major plus. 
Excellent problem-solving and cross-functional collaboration skills. 
Previous experience in a SaaS or B2B setting is highly preferred.
💻 Fully Remote: Work from anywhere with international teams
🚀 Career Growth: Join companies in SaaS, MarTech, and B2B services
🤝 Peer Community: Connect with high-performing sales professionals in our network
🧭 Ongoing Support: Receive guidance from Huzzle before and after placement
💰 Tailored Compensation: Salaries vary by client and candidate preference — we’ll match you with options that fit your goals
ai_core_responsibilities: The GTM Engineer will connect CRM, marketing, and product systems to ensure unified data flow and operational efficiency, while automating lead generation, enrichment, and outreach workflows to reduce manual tasks. Responsibilities also include building data pipelines and analyzing engagement metrics to recommend system improvements.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have experience with CRMs like Salesforce and HubSpot, proficiency in no-code/low-code platforms such as Zapier or Make, and strong analytical skills to interpret performance data. Experience building scalable GTM systems or revenue operations workflows in a SaaS or B2B setting is highly preferred.
linkedin_org_slogan: ChatGPT for Jobs. 100k+ people use Huzzle monthly to find jobs, internships and advance their careers.
linkedin_org_locations: London, England, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Huzzle is an early careers platform connecting students, societies, and employers. Starting out as a CRM for pre-professional student societies, Huzzle has evolved into the university career companion used by over 100,000 talents in the UK.

Research shows that students involved in student initiatives are 3X more hireable, and are rated 18% more career-ready by employers. 

On Huzzle, companies can access these talents by co-hosting events with over 1,000 diverse societies, promoting their roles, and through personalised direct messaging campaigns.

title: Junior Audio Programmer
description_text:
We want you to #JOINTHEREBELLION! 
For 30 years we’ve been independently developing and publishing incredible video games at our multiple studios founded by Jason & Chris Kingsley, but Rebellion is more than just games. We have our own film studio, we create board games, publish books, and through 2000AD, publish comics and graphic novels such as the amazing Judge Dredd.
We are looking for a talented Junior Audio Programmer to join our passionate and experienced Tech Team and be part of one of our exciting new large-scale projects, crafted by a seasoned team renowned for creating exceptional shooters.
Working closely with our in-house tech team, you will help work on our in-house game engine, Asura. The successful candidate will design, implement, and test new audio features while also maintaining and updating the existing codebase. 
Additionally, you will develop and maintain tools that enable designers to create content, collaborating closely with them to introduce new features and identify any issues. You will also work with other teams to support their game development needs.
Responsibilities:
Designing, implementing and testing new audio features in the engine and maintaining and updating the existing code base
Developing and maintaining tools for designers to author content
Working closely with designers to develop new features and identify issues
Collaborating with other teams to meet their game development needs
Research and evaluate new game audio technology
Be a champion for audio in the company
This role is based at our riverside headquarters in Oxford, offering a creative and relaxed atmosphere where teamwork drives our success. We can provide a relocation package, including moving assistance, temporary accommodation, and full support to ensure a smooth transition to your new environment.
Proficient in C++ programming
Passionate about audio
Quick to embrace new processes and workflows
Familiarity with the video game development lifecycle is a plus
Having professional experience in commercial programming or audio is a valuable addition
We offer an extensive benefits package to our team including: 
22 days holidays + Christmas closure (typically 4 days) & Bank holidays (increasing with service at 5 years – one day each year until you reach 31 days)
Private Medical Insurance, healthcare cash plan, including dental and Vision
Life Assurance, Income Protection & Critical Illness Cover
Enhanced Family Leave 
Interest Free, Tax-Free loans
Enhanced Pension Scheme
EAP a range of Mental Health and Wellbeing Support 
Plus loads more… including a wide range of discounts, freebies, and social events! 
Right to Work Statement
This role is only open to applicants who have the permanent right to work in the UK. We are unable to provide or take over visa sponsorship, either now or in the future. Applicants must therefore be able to demonstrate their ongoing eligibility to work in the UK without the need for employer sponsorship.
Life at Our Oxford HQ
Our riverside headquarters in Oxford places you a short walk away from the city centre. Renowned for its rich historical backdrop and esteemed educational institutions, Oxford offers a unique blend of tradition and modernity, with its beautiful architecture and green spaces.
Oxford also boasts excellent transport links, making it easy to navigate both the city and the wider country. The city’s train station, just a short walk from our studio, offers frequent services to London, as well as direct routes to major cities like Birmingham, Manchester, and Reading and we also offer free parking at the studio.
Our open-plan office promotes teamwork and innovation, with regular interaction with senior management and other departments, alongside access to free hot drinks, a handy tuck shop onsite and a range of local pubs, cafes and dining options nearby helping make every workday enjoyable!
Inside Rebellion
Whichever team you join at Rebellion we are proud of our open, friendly, and creative atmosphere. We love what we do, and we have fun doing it! As a big indie developer, we have complete creative freedom over our titles which allows us to work on a massive variety of projects across all the major platforms.
Find out more about us here
Our size, stability, and the variety of in-house expertise across multiple industries, makes Rebellion completely unique! We work in our own way, and we celebrate that as the key to our success. We foster an inclusive culture where you are encouraged to be yourself, to express your creativity and your talent. We are people focused and will support you in every way to can be the best at what you do. We recognise the importance of enjoying what you do and having a healthy work-life balance. We offer a friendly, creative, and relaxed working atmosphere, with sensibly managed projects and a wide variety of benefits and development opportunities for all our teams to ensure you are fully supported with your physical and Mental Well-being.
We are actively recruiting for a diverse team and continue to add to our culture of growth and inclusion. Rebellion encourages innovation in all areas, and we look forward to hearing how you can help us to be better. We encourage all demographics of culture, gender, ethnicity, neurodiversity and beyond, because a representative team makes better products.
If you are interested in working at Rebellion, but this role doesn’t sound quite like you, we’re recruiting for a range of positions across the studio and we’re always happy to receive speculative applications via our website. Visit our Careers website to view all our live vacancies: Rebellion: Careers
#LI-Onsite
ai_core_responsibilities: The successful candidate will be responsible for designing, implementing, and testing new audio features within the in-house game engine, Asura, while also maintaining and updating the existing codebase. Additionally, they will develop and maintain tools for designers to author content and collaborate closely with various teams to support game development needs.
ai_requirements_summary: The primary requirement is proficiency in C++ programming and a strong passion for audio, with the ability to quickly embrace new processes and workflows. Familiarity with the video game development lifecycle and professional experience in commercial programming or audio are considered valuable additions.

linkedin_org_locations: ., Oxford, OX2, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Founded in 1992, Rebellion is one of Europe’s biggest independent multimedia studios. Its flagship Sniper Elite series is renowned for its authentic stealth and sniping gameplay with more than 20 million players worldwide. 2000 AD is synonymous with the absolute finest in British comic book art. With the acquisition of a $100m film studio and the world’s largest archive of English language comics, Rebellion continues to go from strength to strength.

Rebellion now operates across books, comics, TV and film, but at its core it’s still a leading developer and publisher of games. Whether it’s the breathtaking intensity of Sniper Elite 4, the face-hugging terror of Aliens vs. Predator or the grindhouse frenzy of the Zombie Army series, the Oxford-based studio is revered for creating stand-out games designed to thrill.

title: Environment Artist
description_text:
We want you to #JOINTHEREBELLION! 
For 30 years we’ve been independently developing and publishing incredible video games at our multiple studios founded by Jason & Chris Kingsley, but Rebellion is more than just games. We have our own film studio, we create board games, publish books, and through 2000AD, publish comics and graphic novels such as the amazing Judge Dredd. 
We are seeking a passionate and driven Environment Artist to help elevate the visuals of an exciting unannounced game - this is your chance to join a studio famous for the Sniper Elite series, and fresh off the success of Atomfall, our biggest game launch to date.
In this position, you will create props and environments in a realistic art style for one of our unannounced projects, while taking ownership of entire levels. You’ll collaborate across departments, produce benchmark-quality assets, and work proactively from loose briefs that require independent research and 3D prototyping.
Artistic background, enthusiastic attitude, and strong communication skills are essential for success in this role. You will have the opportunity to shape the project in meaningful ways and therefore enjoy a high level of autonomy.
This role is based at our riverside headquarters in Oxford, offering a creative and relaxed atmosphere where teamwork drives our success. We can provide a relocation package, including moving assistance, temporary accommodation, and full support to ensure a smooth transition to your new environment.
Responsibilities: 
Planning and building entire levels together with level design, with consideration of visual storytelling and memorable aesthetics. 
Create functional assets which can be shared across the project in respect of the technical and artistic constraints
Track and update the progress of tasks using the designated tracking tools
Communicate and collaborate with key stakeholders to confirm task direction and consistency of own work
Ensure the technical and artistic quality of the project by collaborating with Technical Artists and the Environment Art Team, and make changes when necessary
Proven experience in professional game development with a good understanding of environment art pipelines.
Portfolio showcasing strong, realistic props and environments presented in-engine, demonstrating solid composition, lighting, and materials. 
Strong art fundamentals (composition, colour, scale, shape language, lighting) with the ability to explain what’s working and suggest improvements. 
Able to iterate efficiently in 3D, progressing from blockout to polished set-dressing.
Demonstrates originality and good reference skills, and refines work through iteration (not just replication). 
Ideally confident in Blender; experience with Maya, 3ds Max, Houdini, or similar DCC tools also welcomed. 
Working knowledge of PBR workflows, including Substance Painter and high-to-low baking techniques. 
Comfortable building and optimising environments in a professional game engine, balancing visual quality with performance constraints.
We offer an extensive benefits package to our team including: 
22 days holidays + Christmas closure (typically 4 days) & Bank holidays (increasing with service at 5 years – one day each year until you reach 31 days)
Private Medical Insurance, healthcare cash plan, including dental and Vision
Life Assurance, Income Protection & Critical Illness Cover
Enhanced Family Leave 
Interest Free, Tax-Free loans
Enhanced Pension Scheme
EAP a range of Mental Health and Wellbeing Support 
Plus loads more… including a wide range of discounts, freebies, and social events!
