title: Technical Lead
description_text:
About zaimler

AI agents can't reason over data they don't understand. Enterprise data today is fragmented across dozens of systems with no shared context, meaning, or structure, and that's why most enterprise AI is failing. The shift from copilots to autonomous agents is creating an entirely new infrastructure layer, and we're building it.

zaimler is the context infrastructure for the agentic era: a platform that automatically discovers domain knowledge, maps relationships, and gives AI agents the semantic understanding to operate with precision at scale. Imagine knowledge graphs that support real-time inference, built for systems that need to reason, not just retrieve.

zaimler was founded by Biswajit Das (ex-VP Engineering, Truera), a Data Infra veteran and former Chief Architect at Visa, and Sofus Macskassy (ex-Director of Engineering, LinkedIn), who built one of the largest knowledge graphs in production in the industry at LinkedIn. We're a small, senior team at the seed stage, deploying with major enterprises across insurance, travel, and technology. If you want to build infrastructure that the next decade of AI runs on, we'd love to talk.

The Role

As a Technical Lead, you’ll sit at the intersection of technical execution, architecture, and leadership.

This is a hands-on role. You’ll write code, own systems, and lead our growing team of engineers, helping us ship production-quality infrastructure while setting a high bar for engineering rigor as we scale.

You are not a project manager. You are a builder who can lead.

What You'll Do
\n

Technical Leadership
Own the design and implementation of core backend/platform systems
Lead architecture discussions and make pragmatic tradeoffs in ambiguous environments
Set technical direction for a small team while staying deeply involved in the codebase
Review PRs with an eye toward correctness, performance, and long-term maintainability
Drive technical decisions across data, infra, and application layers as needed

Team Leadership
Manage and mentor a small group of engineers 
Provide regular feedback, coaching, and growth guidance
Help onboard new engineers and set expectations for technical excellence
Foster a culture of ownership, velocity, and thoughtful engineering

Execution & Delivery
Break down complex problems into clear milestones
Balance speed with system quality in a fast-moving startup environment
Partner closely with product and leadership to align technical work with company goals
Identify risks early and proactively unblock the team

What We're Looking For
Strong experience as a senior or staff-level engineer in production systems
Prior experience leading engineers, formally or informally
Comfort owning systems end-to-end: design → build → deploy → operate
Strong fundamentals in:Distributed systemsBackend servicesAPIs and data flows
Excellent judgment in ambiguous, fast-moving environments
Willingness to be hands-on, this role writes real code

Nice to Have
Experience in early-stage startups
Background in data infrastructure, ML platforms, or cloud-native systems
Experience scaling systems or teams from “small but working” to “reliable at scale”
Comfort working across infra, backend, and application layers

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Technical Lead, you will own the design and implementation of core backend/platform systems while leading a small team of engineers. You will be responsible for driving technical decisions and ensuring high engineering standards as the team scales.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should have strong experience as a senior or staff-level engineer and prior experience leading engineers. A solid understanding of distributed systems, backend services, and a willingness to be hands-on in coding are essential.

title: Power BI Engineer (QuickSight)
description_text:
Lab49 is an award-winning specialist consultancy that creates bespoke technology in partnership with the most important companies in finance. Founded in 2002, we bring Silicon Valley innovation to global financial institutions and have delivered hundreds of projects for tier-one investment banks and leading buy-side firms worldwide.

Being part of Lab49 means working in high-impact, cross-functional teams, consulting directly with clients, and delivering production-grade technology solutions that solve real business problems.

Job Summary:
Working with leading global financial institutions as part of our analytics and reporting teams in Noida, you will design, build, and optimize enterprise BI and reporting solutions using Power BI, Amazon QuickSight, and Jasper Reports to enable data-driven decision-making and operational excellence.
\n

Responsibilities
Design and develop robust, scalable, and high-performance BI solutions using Power BI, QuickSight, and Jasper Reports
Build interactive dashboards, analytical reports, and pixel-perfect operational reports
Retrieve, transform, and model complex datasets using SQL, Power Query (M), and ETL processes
Optimize data models and reports for performance, scalability, and usability
Leverage DAX and advanced visualization techniques to deliver actionable insights
Collaborate closely with data engineers, business analysts, and stakeholders to gather requirements and deliver high-quality solutions

Required Experience Skills:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field
5+ years of hands-on experience with Power BI (DAX, data modelling, Power Query)
Mandatory: Strong experience with Jasper Reports (report design, parameters, sub-reports)
Hands-on experience with Amazon QuickSight for cloud-based analytics
Strong SQL skills and experience with relational databases
Solid understanding of data modelling and data visualization best practices
Excellent communication and stakeholder collaboration skills

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves designing, building, and optimizing enterprise BI and reporting solutions using Power BI, Amazon QuickSight, and Jasper Reports. The engineer will collaborate with data engineers and business analysts to deliver high-quality solutions that enable data-driven decision-making.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree in a related field and at least 5 years of hands-on experience with Power BI and Jasper Reports. Strong SQL skills and experience with Amazon QuickSight are also required.

title: International Tax Analyst
description_text:
About the Role

As an International Tax Analyst, you will support the company’s international tax matters, including compliance, reporting, and ongoing tax-related activities. This role is responsible for preparing and validating tax data, supporting tax-related transactions, and assisting with day-to-day international tax operations to ensure accuracy and regulatory compliance.
\n

What You Will Do
Assist in all tax compliance workstreams and statutory reporting for Singapore operations for corporate income tax, withholding tax, and indirect taxes, including: preparing tax returns and income tax provisions; conducting regular health checks; extracting reports from accounting systems; analyzing and reconciling numbers from different sources; tax accounting reconciliation; and working closely with finance and transfer pricing teams
May support in adhoc compliance workstreams for China operations
Coordinate cross-border transactions to ensure appropriate tax treatment and alignment with group policies.
Support with tax audits and due dilligences by working with internal stakeholders and external advisors to gather information and address tax-related queries.
Assist with research and implementation of various tax initiatives which includes working closely with stakeholders to understand the tax impact of business operations and ensure that tax needs are identified, considered and communicated
Monitor changes in Singapore tax regulations and support timely implementation and documentation under guidance.
Maintain accurate records and documentation for tax and compliance purposes.

What You Will Need
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Taxation, or a related field. 
2–3 years of corporate tax and / or indirect tax experience in a Big 4 firm and/ or a commercial environment (international exposure is an advantage).
Strong financial acumen, analytical skills and attention to detail
Proficient in English, both written and verbal; ability to communicate clearly with regional stakeholders.
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, high-growth, and evolving environment.
Able to work independently with guidance, as well as collaboratively within a team.

\n
About the Team

The Tax – International team works closely with stakeholders across the business to support tax compliance, advisory, and cross-border activities. As a core member of the team, you will collaborate with internal partners to ensure international tax requirements are met effectively.

About GoTo Group
GoTo is the largest digital ecosystem in Indonesia. GoTo's mission is to 'empower progress' by offering technology infrastructure and solutions that help everyone to access and thrive in the digital economy.
The GoTo ecosystem provides a wide range of services, including mobility, delivery, payments, financial services, and technology solutions for merchants. The ecosystem also provides e-commerce services through Tokopedia and banking services through its partnership with Bank Jago.

About Gojek 
Gojek is Southeast Asia’s leading on-demand platform and pioneer of the multi-service ecosystem with over 2.5 million driver partners across the regions offering a wide range of services such as transportation, food delivery, logistics and more. With its mission to create impact at scale, Gojek is committed to resolving consumer problems and raising standards of living by connecting consumers to the best providers of goods and services in the market.

About GoTo Financial
GoTo Financial accelerates financial inclusion through its leading financial services and merchants solutions. Its consumer services include GoPay and GoPayLater and serve businesses of all sizes through Midtrans, Moka, GoBiz Plus, GoBiz, and Selly. With its trusted and inclusive ecosystem of products, GoTo Financial is open to new growth opportunities and aims to empower everyone to Make It Happen, Make It Together, Make It Last.

GoTo and its business units, including Gojek and GoToFinancial ("GoTo") only post job opportunities on our official channels on our respective company websites and on LinkedIn. GoTo is not liable for any job postings or job offers that did not originate from us. You should conduct your own due diligence to prevent being victims of any fake job scams, if they did not originate from GoTo's official recruitment channels.



#LI-HYBRID

title: Car Fleet Specialist (Poland)
description_text:
Ajax Systems is an international tech company and the largest manufacturer of security systems in Europe, with over 4.5 million end users and 330K PRO users in more than 180 countries worldwide. The company offers end-to-end solutions for protecting homes and businesses оf any scale. To date, Ajax product portfolio includes 280 devices for intrusion protection, video surveillance, fire and life safety, and comfort and automation. The company employs 5, 000 specialists. 

We develop local teams because we understand local experts best comprehend market specifics. The company has sales representatives worldwide, including Italy, Spain, Germany, France, the United Kingdom, the USA, Australia, South Africa and other regions. The Global Business Development team is like-minded people who share the ambitious goals of Ajax Systems. 

The best software combined with powerful hardware has forever changed the perception of security systems for users and industry professionals. Being recognized at the biggest specialized exhibitions such as IFSEC, Expoprotection and Intersec, as well as winning prestigious awards from industry-leading media like PSI magazine and GIT SECURITY, today Ajax is one of the most awarded security systems in Europe.

Ajax Systems production includes four factories, two of which are located in Ukraine, one in Turkey and one in Vietnam. At the same time, the company's certification according to the ISO 14001 standard ensures a minimal impact of production on the environment. The selection of components for Ajax device manufacturing is determined by the presence of manufacturer declarations confirming compliance with RoHS requirements.

The company emerged against the backdrop of revolution and war. That's why resilience is the cornerstone of the brand's DNA, and the fight against evil by creating the best security and automation devices is Ajax unwavering mission.

\n

Key requirements:
1 year of experience on similar position.
Proficiency in the Microsoft Office suite, particularly Excel.
Foreign language: English – fluent, C1/C2 level.
Knowledge of short- and long-term leasing conditions in European Union countries.
Ability to effectively organize one’s own work and high communication skills.
Proactive approach to seeking new solutions.

Responsibilities: 
Coordinating the fleet across the European Union (including Poland)
Verifying fleet service agreements in individual EU countries
Ensuring operational activities comply with applicable fleet service agreements across various EU countries.
Opening new credit lines and negotiating financial terms, including fees, repayment schedules, deposits, and prepayments.
Verifying mileage and handling claims under existing agreements.
Building and developing the current fleet.
Vehicle allocation and supervision
Allocating company vehicles to employees based on role requirements and needs.
Monitoring compliance with fleet policy and legal regulations.
Maintaining records of vehicle operating costs and coordinating maintenance services. Providing daily support to vehicle users, including assistance with claims and service issues.
Ongoing communication with drivers to ensure operational support.
Developing and implementing car policy
Developing fleet management procedures.
Overseeing vehicle insurance and ensuring all fleet operations meet regulatory standards.
Budgeting and planning car fleet costs
Planning and controlling the car fleet budget, including determining optimal expenditures related to the maintenance and operation of company vehicles. Preparing reports and analyses on profitability.
Monitoring both fixed and variable costs.
Monthly verification of incoming invoices related to the car fleet.

\n
We get to work every day and build a better security world for people. At Ajax Systems, we are all a part of something bigger than ourselves. Join us and be part of Ajax Systems company, where you will feel empowered to succeed!

title: Product Designer, Content Creation
description_text:
Working at Omnisend is... kind of boring. Not the soul-crushing kind of boring — more like the “no drama, no chaos, just smart people doing great work” kind. The kind that lets you focus on what matters, without pointless meetings, micromanagement, or bureaucracy. We’re data-driven. We test, we iterate, we improve. Gut feelings don’t drive decisions — insights do. We trust each other. We’re transparent, give real feedback, and actually listen. No egos, no hand-holding — just a team of experienced professionals moving fast, adapting easily, and delivering impact. Omnisend is customer-funded and customer-driven. Our work is rooted in solving real problems that help ecommerce brands grow. No empty promises — we bring in results. And yes, we have fun. But the grown-up kind — workations, team gatherings, the occasional party. No forced fun, no awkward icebreakers. Just genuine moments with a team that actually enjoys working together.

Your role: You’ll join the Content Creation team with a clear mission: to help ecommerce businesses create and send beautiful, on-brand, and personalized emails that drive real growth. At the heart of this role is empowering merchants to make their marketing both visually stunning and deeply relevant — turning great ideas into emails that truly connect with their audience.
\n

What you’ll do @Omnisend:
Take ownership of the user experience within your product area—shaping it end-to-end with clarity and intent.
Translate insights from research, product data, and strategy into compelling, user-centered design solutions.
Drive iterative design decisions that balance user needs, technical realities, and business outcomes.
Champion design quality and consistency across all product surfaces and customer touchpoints.
Raise the bar for product design, contributing to a culture of craft, critique, and continuous improvement.
Advance our design system, ensuring it supports scalable, efficient delivery across teams.

We expect that you’ll bring:
3+ years of hands-on product design experience (UX & UI) in user-centric tech environments, with a portfolio of shipped design work.
Strong skills in interaction design, lo-fi and hi-fi prototyping.
A customer-first mindset and the ability to translate insights into thoughtful, intuitive solutions.
Hands-on experience with user research—planning, conducting, and applying both moderated and unmoderated methods.
Ability to clearly communicate design rationale and advocate for users in cross-functional discussions.
Strategic thinking that connects design decisions to user needs, product goals, and long-term impact.
Understanding of how design systems are built, maintained, and scaled across teams.
Proficiency in Figma; experience with tools like Mixpanel is a plus.
Proven experience working within multidisciplinary product teams.
Strong sense of ownership, initiative, and a collaborative approach to problem-solving.
Experience mentoring other designers and/or leading initiatives is a strong advantage.

How we work and what we value:
Aligned and focused – Company-wide OKRs and the GIST framework keep us working toward clear, meaningful outcomes.
Experiment-driven – We ship quickly to learn, test ideas early, and improve based on real feedback.
Customer and data-informed – We talk to users regularly and use product metrics to guide our decisions.
Product-led growth – We believe the product experience should drive adoption, retention, and long-term success.

What we offer in return:
Gross salary starting at 4000 EUR/month based on experience.
Quarterly salary reviews.
An unlimited learning budget for self-improvement complying with the best interest of Omnisend
Working methods and best practices inspired by the best tech companies in Silicon Valley
Over 80% senior colleagues in Product - and over half company-wide - to accelerate your growth
Flexible working hours and remote work possibilities
Private health insurance
Unlimited access to psychotherapy
Workstation budget
Personalized work anniversary gifts (house cleaning, spa treatments, international flights etc.)
Annual company trip abroad, company gatherings twice a year, team-led teambuildings
Additional days off for KASP and Rifleman’s Union members

\n
Omnisend is an email and SMS marketing platform built for ecommerce brands. With one-click integration to major ecommerce platforms, intuitive automation, and powerful email templates, we make sophisticated marketing effortless — no learning curve required.

Award-winning 24/7/365 customer support? Check. A platform trusted by growing brands and seasoned pros alike? Absolutely. The idea that “great marketing needs to be expensive”? Our 150,000+ customers prove that wrong every day.
ai_benefits: Additional Days Off for KASP and Rifleman’s Union Members
ai_core_responsibilities: You will take ownership of the user experience within your product area, shaping it end-to-end. Your mission is to help ecommerce businesses create and send beautiful, on-brand, and personalized emails that drive real growth.
ai_requirements_summary: The role requires 3+ years of hands-on product design experience in user-centric tech environments, with strong skills in interaction design and prototyping. You should have a customer-first mindset and the ability to translate insights into intuitive solutions.
linkedin_org_slogan: As a business owner, you have enough “exciting” stuff happening. Why add more? Choose stable & effective instead.

linkedin_org_description:
Let’s be real. Email marketing is not the most exciting member of the marketing family. And that’s okay. As a business owner, you have enough “exciting” stuff happening. Who wants more excitement when you can have stable and effective instead? Let us be boring, in the best way possible.  

• No need to learn anything new here. A platform so boringly intuitive, it’s like you designed it yourself. 

• No venturing into the great unknown. Our support team is available 24/7 to make sure you don’t have any unwanted adventures.

• Same same, but for less. Get everything you expect from other platforms, except for those rollercoaster prices that make your heart race.

• No guessing whether that marketing campaign will pay off. Brands using Omnisend on average make $68 for every $1 spent (based on data from Omnisend's US customers in 2023 and 2024).

title: Senior GL Accountant
description_text:
Contentsquare is the all-in-one experience intelligence platform designed to be easily used by anyone who cares about digital journeys. With our flexible and scalable platform, organizations quickly get a deep understanding of their customers’ whole online journey.

We are a global leader in the experience analytics space, with a growing presence across 15 offices worldwide. We’re here to stay—and we’re looking for team members who are excited to drive impact and help us scale even further.

Our aim is to create an inclusive workplace where everyone learns and succeeds. Contentsquare has built a community of individuals who are daring, understanding, and deliberate. We invite you to join us in making the complex simpler—for our customers, their customers, and each other.

Important note: Be careful of scammers pretending to be from Contentsquare. We will never ask for money or contact you through random texts. For more information, visit our careers blog.

About the job
Yu will be responsible for a range of General Accounting activities for several Contentsquare Group entities, in compliance with IFRS and local GAAP.

What you’ll do
Support day-to-day general ledger operations, including posting journal entries and performing account reconciliations.
Contribute to monthly, quarterly, and year-end closing activities, ensuring compliance with IFRS and local GAAP requirements.
Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual tax filings and support statutory audit processes.
Maintain accurate accounting records for prepaid expenses, accruals, and fixed assets.
Participate in producing internal and external financial reporting.
Partner cross-functionally with teams such as FP&A, Legal, HR, and Procurement to ensure accurate inputs and smooth processes.
Contribute to process improvement initiatives and support ERP enhancements.
Support the implementation, documentation, and ongoing maintenance of accounting policies and internal controls.

What you’ll need to succeed
Advanced degree in Accounting.
7–10 years’ minimum experience in General Accounting (IFRS required; UK GAAP is a plus), ideally with exposure to both an accounting firm and an in-house corporate environment.
Fluency in English (another European language is a plus) to operate in a truly international environment.
Strong analytical mindset with solid technical accounting expertise.
Proactive self-starter who is disciplined, organized, thorough, and able to work autonomously with strong attention to detail and a collaborative team approach.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Demonstrated ability to multitask and prioritize in a fast-paced environment, perform under pressure, and meet non-negotiable deadlines.

Nice to have
Experience in the Tech / SaaS sector (beneficial, not required).
Strong Excel skills, including VBA.
Experience with Workday Financials.
\n

\n
Why you should join Contentsquare
We invest in our people through career development, mentorship, social events, philanthropic activities, and competitive benefits. We are always assessing the perks we offer to ensure we’re aligned with the employees' needs.

Here are a few we want to highlight:
- Virtual onboarding, Hackathon, and various opportunities to interact with your team and global colleagues both on and offsite each year
- Work flexibility: hybrid and remote work policies
- Generous paid time-off policy (every location is different)
- Lifestyle allowance
- A Culture Crew in every country we’re based in to coordinate regular activities for employees to get to know each other and bond outside of work
- Every full-time employee receives stock options, allowing them to share in the company’s success
- We have multiple Employee Resource Groups, that offer a safe space for individuals who share common identities, life experiences, or allyship to connect, support one another, and passionately advocate for the issues close to their hearts
- And more benefits tailored to each country

Contentsquare is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Your personal data is used by Contentsquare for recruitment purposes only. Read our Job Candidate Privacy Notice to find out more about data protection at Contentsquare and your rights. You can exercise your rights by using our dedicated Data Subject Rights Portal here. 

Your personal data will be securely stored in our hosting provider’s data center in Oregon (US west). We have implemented appropriate transfer mechanisms under applicable data protection laws.

Contentsquare may use AI-assisted tools to help review and screen applications. All decisions involving hiring are made by human reviewers, and your personal data will be processed in accordance with our Candidate Privacy Policy.
ai_core_responsibilities: You will be responsible for a range of General Accounting activities for several Contentsquare Group entities, ensuring compliance with IFRS and local GAAP. This includes supporting day-to-day general ledger operations, contributing to closing activities, and maintaining accurate accounting records.
ai_requirements_summary: An advanced degree in Accounting and 7-10 years of experience in General Accounting are required, with IFRS knowledge being essential. Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and excellent communication abilities are also necessary for success in this role.

title: Revenue Operations Analyst
description_text:
Rockstar is recruiting for a forward-thinking legal technology company that is transforming the commercial real estate industry. This client is on a mission to make real estate transactions smarter, faster, and friction-free by providing an AI assistant built exclusively for commercial real estate law. The team is reimagining how transactions get done, moving fast, working collaboratively, and empowering individuals to make a significant impact from day one.
🚀 The company is on a mission to make real estate transactions smarter, faster, and friction-free.
🏢 Real estate is the world’s largest asset class, yet the legal processes and tools behind it remain slow, manual, and underinvested. Lawyers must review dense documents line by line and piece together information across silos, all while clients demand faster, more transparent due diligence.
🤖 That is where the company comes in. Its AI assistant accelerates complex due diligence by up to 70% while delivering legal-grade precision. It was developed with former practicing real estate lawyers.
🤝 The company is trusted by leading firms to remove the busywork so legal teams can focus on what they do best: applying sharp legal judgment, delivering standout client service, and getting deals over the line faster.
💡 Working at the company means joining a team that is reimagining how real estate transactions get done - moving fast, working collaboratively, and giving people the ownership to make a real impact from day one.
Role Overview
The company is seeking a highly analytical and systems-oriented Revenue Operations Analyst to support and scale its go-to-market (GTM) engine. This role sits at the center of Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success and is critical to ensuring clean data, efficient processes, accurate reporting, and aligned incentives across the revenue organization.
This position has a strong focus on HubSpot administration, including data hygiene, workflow automation, and system optimization. The successful candidate will also partner closely with Sales leadership on compensation planning, territory management, and day-to-day account executive (AE) support.
What You’ll Do
HubSpot Administration & Optimization (Core Focus)
- Own day-to-day HubSpot administration across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success hubs
- Maintain high standards of data cleanliness and integrity, including deduplication, normalization, and lifecycle management
- Design, build, and maintain HubSpot workflows, automation, and routing logic
- Manage custom objects, properties, permissions, and integrations
- Proactively identify and resolve system issues, inefficiencies, and data gaps
- Document HubSpot processes and establish best practices for GTM teams
Reporting & Analytics
- Build and maintain dashboards and reports for Sales, Marketing, and Leadership
- Deliver accurate reporting on pipeline, forecast, bookings, conversion rates, and GTM performance
- Partner with stakeholders to define metrics, KPIs, and reporting requirements
- Ensure reporting consistency across tools and teams
GTM Compensation & Planning
- Support sales compensation planning and administration, including quota and commission tracking
- Partner with Finance and Sales leadership to ensure compensation accuracy and transparency
- Assist with modeling and analysis for compensation plan changes
Account Executive Support
- Act as a trusted operational partner to AEs by resolving CRM issues and improving workflows
- Support lead/account routing, pipeline hygiene, and opportunity management
- Provide ad hoc analysis and operational support to help AEs close deals faster
Territory & Account Management
- Support territory design and management, including account segmentation and assignment
- Partner with Sales leadership on territory changes, coverage models, and capacity planning
- Ensure territories and account ownership are accurately reflected in HubSpot
You’ll Excel Here If You Have
Required
- 3+ years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or Business Operations at a B2B SaaS company
- Hands-on HubSpot administration experience (required), ideally experience with Salesforce too
- Proven experience with HubSpot data clean-up, deduplication, and lifecycle management
- Strong experience building and maintaining HubSpot workflows and automation
- Advanced reporting and dashboarding experience in HubSpot (and/or BI tools)
- Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
- Experience supporting sales teams, including AEs and sales leadership
- Excellent attention to detail and process-oriented mindset
Preferred
- HubSpot Admin or HubSpot Operations Hub certification
- Salesforce knowledge
- Experience supporting GTM compensation and commission processes
- Experience with territory planning and account segmentation
- Familiarity with additional GTM tools (e.g., Salesforce, Outreach, Gong, ZoomInfo, Snowflake)
- Experience in a high-growth or scaling SaaS environment
What Success Looks Like
- HubSpot is trusted as a clean, reliable source of truth across GTM teams
- Sales and leadership have clear, accurate reporting and insights
- AEs spend less time on operational friction and more time selling
- Compensation, territories, and account ownership are accurate and well-managed
- Revenue processes scale smoothly as the company grows
Benefits
- 25 days paid holiday (plus bank holidays)
- Professional equipment and personal development budget along with training opportunities to learn and develop your skills
- Cycle-to-work scheme
- Matched pension contributions and equity options in a fast growing start-up
- An inclusive community enjoying all-company off-sites, lunches and socials
🔒 Security is everyone’s responsibility at the company. All team members are asked to follow security policies, complete regular awareness training, and handle sensitive data with care in line with ISO 27001 standards. Reporting risks or incidents quickly helps maintain the strong culture of security and compliance everyone depends on.
💡 The company is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. It especially welcomes applications from people who are traditionally underrepresented in tech. Even if a candidate does not meet every single requirement, or if the right role is not listed yet, the company would still love to hear from them.
💰 This hiring range is a reasonable estimate of the base pay range for this position at the time of posting. Pay is based on several factors, which may include job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and business requirements.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Revenue Operations Analyst will support and scale the company's go-to-market engine by ensuring clean data, efficient processes, and accurate reporting. This role involves HubSpot administration, reporting and analytics, compensation planning, and account executive support.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3+ years of experience in Revenue Operations or related fields, with hands-on HubSpot administration experience. Strong analytical skills and experience supporting sales teams are also required.

Founded by entrepreneur Eddie Phanichkul in 2014, the Rockstar Art & Science Education Advancement Foundation is a future 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation based in San Diego, CA.

Our immediate mission is to use music to educate underprivileged children. We’ll do this by creating hands-on experiences and performances for children to get them excited about music, and then ultimately science and arts and beyond.

Our secondary mission is to create grant and scholarship programs designed to help youth and young adults afford tools for science, technology, art, and music, while offering low-cost or no-cost workshops designed to reinforce and educate youth and young adults on conceptual science, technology, art, and music.

title: Global Event Marketing Manager (H/F) - Fixed term Contract (CDD)
description_text:
A2MAC1 is the leading provider of automotive benchmarking and data analytics worldwide. Our solutions help OEMs and suppliers understand their competitive landscape, accelerate development, and make better strategic decisions based on reliable, structured, and actionable insights. Through our digital platforms, data services, consulting expertise, and AI-driven solutions, A2MAC1 supports the world’s top automotive players. With 700+ employees across Europe, Asia, and North America, A2MAC1 continues its strong growth, driven by innovation and customer success.
We are looking for a Global Event Manager (H/F) to support and execute A2MAC1’s global event strategy during a key period. This role focuses on operational excellence, coordination, and execution of B2B events across Europe and selected international markets.
The position plays a critical role in ensuring smooth delivery of events while working closely with marketing, sales, and external partners
KEY RESPONSABILITIES
Global Event Planning & Execution
· Plan, coordinate, and execute A2MAC1’s B2B events (trade shows, conferences, customer events, workshops).
· Manage end-to-end event logistics: venue coordination, suppliers, booth setup, materials, and on-site operations.
· Ensure events are delivered on time, on budget, and aligned with brand and business objectives.
Budget & Vendor Management
· Manage and track event budgets in coordination with the marketing team.
· Coordinate with external vendors and service providers (booth builders, logistics partners, agencies).
· Ensure cost efficiency and proper follow-up on invoices and contracts.
Internal Coordination
· Work closely with Marketing, Sales, Product Marketing, and Design teams to align event objectives and content.
· Coordinate event-related materials (booths, presentations, giveaways, logistics assets).
· Support post-event follow-up and feedback collection.
On-site Event Support
· Travel to key events in Europe (and occasionally internationally) to ensure smooth on-site execution.
· Regular travel to our Hary site is required to support event logistics, preparation, and on-site coordination.
· Act as the main operational point of contact during events.
Process & Continuous Improvement
· Contribute to improving event processes, tools, and best practices.
· Support event reporting and ROI tracking in collaboration with internal stakeholders.
Professional Background
· 4–6 years of experience in event management, ideally in a B2B or international environment.
· Proven experience managing multiple events simultaneously.
· Experience working with external vendors and agencies.
Skills & Abilities
· Strong organizational and project management skills.
· Hands-on, solution-oriented mindset with strong attention to detail.
· Ability to work autonomously while collaborating with cross-functional teams.
· Comfortable managing operational topics in fast-paced environments.
· Excellent communication skills in English (written and spoken); French is a strong plus.
· Willingness to travel as required for events.
Nice to Have
· Experience in industrial, automotive, or technology-driven environments.
· Familiarity with CRM or event tracking tools.
· Interest in branding, booth design, and event experience optimization.
ai_core_responsibilities: This role is responsible for the end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of A2MAC1’s global B2B events, including managing logistics, budgets, and external vendors across Europe and international markets. The manager will also ensure alignment with brand objectives, support internal teams, and provide critical on-site support during events.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess 4–6 years of experience in event management, preferably in a B2B or international setting, with proven ability to manage multiple simultaneous events and external agencies. Essential skills include strong organizational and project management capabilities, autonomy, cross-functional collaboration, and excellent English communication.

A2MAC1 is a leading global provider of benchmarking and engineering insights, dedicated to decoding the future of the automotive and mobility industries. Since its inception, A2MAC1 has been at the forefront of innovation, helping clients navigate the complexities of the evolving market through comprehensive data analysis and strategic expertise.
Our mission is to empower businesses with the knowledge and tools they need to make informed decisions, drive innovation, and achieve sustainable growth. We offer a unique blend of technical insights, competitive analysis, and trend forecasting, tailored to meet the specific needs of our clients.
A2MAC1’s extensive database and advanced analytical tools provide unparalleled insights into vehicle architecture, technology, and performance. Our team of experts works closely with clients to deliver productivity solutions that enhance product development, reduce engineering cycle times and optimize costs, and ultimately improve overall market positioning.
Committed to advancing the future of mobility, A2MAC1 collaborates with industry leaders, leveraging our deep understanding of automotive engineering to foster innovation and support the transition to sustainable and accessible transportation solutions.
With a global presence and a reputation for excellence, A2MAC1 is the trusted partner for companies looking to stay ahead in a rapidly changing industry. We decode the future, enabling our clients to engineer and build the next generation of automotive and mobility solutions, better, faster and more sustainable.

title: GTM Engineer
description_text:
About Huzzle
At Huzzle, we connect high-performing B2B sales professionals with global companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include startups, digital agencies, and tech platforms in industries like SaaS, MarTech, FinTech, and EdTech. We match top sales talent to full-time remote roles where they’re hired directly into client teams and provided ongoing support by Huzzle.
Role Type: Full-time
Engagement: Independent Contractor 
Hours: UK Timezone 
Job Summary
We’re looking for a GTM Engineer—a hybrid strategist and technologist who integrates sales, marketing, and product platforms to drive efficient and scalable revenue operations. If you love solving system bottlenecks, building automations, and working at the intersection of data and business, this role is for you.
Key Responsibilities
Connect CRM, marketing, and product systems to ensure unified data flow and operational efficiency 
Automate lead generation, enrichment, and outreach workflows to reduce manual tasks and increase productivity 
Build and manage data pipelines that support real-time visibility into sales and marketing performance 
Analyze engagement metrics and GTM workflows to identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements 
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business objectives into scalable technical solutions 
Continuously test, optimize, and refine GTM systems to support faster, smarter revenue operations
Experience with CRMs like Salesforce, HubSpot, and marketing automation tools. 
Proficiency in no-code/low-code platforms (Zapier, Make, Tray.io, Airtable, etc.). 
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret performance data and generate strategic insights. 
Experience building scalable GTM systems or revenue operations workflows is a major plus. 
Excellent problem-solving and cross-functional collaboration skills. 
Previous experience in a SaaS or B2B setting is highly preferred.
💻 Fully Remote: Work from anywhere with international teams
🚀 Career Growth: Join companies in SaaS, MarTech, and B2B services
🤝 Peer Community: Connect with high-performing sales professionals in our network
🧭 Ongoing Support: Receive guidance from Huzzle before and after placement
💰 Tailored Compensation: Salaries vary by client and candidate preference — we’ll match you with options that fit your goals
ai_core_responsibilities: The GTM Engineer will connect CRM, marketing, and product systems to ensure unified data flow and operational efficiency. They will also automate workflows, build data pipelines, and collaborate with teams to optimize GTM systems.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience with CRMs like Salesforce and HubSpot, as well as proficiency in no-code/low-code platforms. Strong analytical skills and previous experience in a SaaS or B2B setting are highly preferred.
linkedin_org_slogan: ChatGPT for Jobs. 100k+ people use Huzzle monthly to find jobs, internships and advance their careers.
linkedin_org_locations: London, England, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Huzzle is an early careers platform connecting students, societies, and employers. Starting out as a CRM for pre-professional student societies, Huzzle has evolved into the university career companion used by over 100,000 talents in the UK.

Research shows that students involved in student initiatives are 3X more hireable, and are rated 18% more career-ready by employers. 

On Huzzle, companies can access these talents by co-hosting events with over 1,000 diverse societies, promoting their roles, and through personalised direct messaging campaigns.

· Hands-on, solution-oriented mindset with strong attention to detail.
· Ability to work autonomously while collaborating with cross-functional teams.
· Comfortable managing operational topics in fast-paced environments.
· Excellent communication skills in English (written and spoken); French is a strong plus.
· Willingness to travel as required for events.
Nice to Have
· Experience in industrial, automotive, or technology-driven environments.
· Familiarity with CRM or event tracking tools.
· Interest in branding, booth design, and event experience optimization.

linkedin_org_description:
A2MAC1 is a leading global provider of benchmarking and engineering insights, dedicated to decoding the future of the automotive and mobility industries. Since its inception, A2MAC1 has been at the forefront of innovation, helping clients navigate the complexities of the evolving market through comprehensive data analysis and strategic expertise.
Our mission is to empower businesses with the knowledge and tools they need to make informed decisions, drive innovation, and achieve sustainable growth. We offer a unique blend of technical insights, competitive analysis, and trend forecasting, tailored to meet the specific needs of our clients.
A2MAC1’s extensive database and advanced analytical tools provide unparalleled insights into vehicle architecture, technology, and performance. Our team of experts works closely with clients to deliver productivity solutions that enhance product development, reduce engineering cycle times and optimize costs, and ultimately improve overall market positioning.
Committed to advancing the future of mobility, A2MAC1 collaborates with industry leaders, leveraging our deep understanding of automotive engineering to foster innovation and support the transition to sustainable and accessible transportation solutions.
With a global presence and a reputation for excellence, A2MAC1 is the trusted partner for companies looking to stay ahead in a rapidly changing industry. We decode the future, enabling our clients to engineer and build the next generation of automotive and mobility solutions, better, faster and more sustainable.

title: Brand Strategist Lead
description_text:
ComeOn Group in short
Founded in 2008, ComeOn Group is a leading global player in the rapidly growing iGaming market. Having launched its first online brand in 2010, ComeOn Group is running a wide range of exciting brands across multiple markets. Operating more than 20 brands on its proprietary platform providing a secure, innovative and entertainment-led player experience across casino and sports betting. ComeOn Group is an international employer within the iGaming industry and has over 500 talented employees across 7 main locations. 
Job Overview
As the Brand Strategist Lead, you will take a senior role in defining and steering the long-term strategic direction for our diverse portfolio of brands. You will act as a pivotal link between high-level company objectives and operational execution, ensuring our multi-brand strategy is robust, innovative, and consistently applied. This role requires a blend of visionary thinking and hands-on guidance, as you will mentor Brand and Country Managers to optimize brand development, highlight emerging opportunities, and navigate market challenges. Beyond consumer brands, you will lead the governance of the company’s internal brand strategy in close partnership with our HR teams to foster a unified corporate culture.
Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership: Define and oversee the implementation of long-term brand strategies for all consumer brands, ensuring alignment with the global multi-brand framework.
Market Intelligence & Reporting: Lead the measurement, analysis, and dissemination of reports regarding market share, demographics, and advertising impact to inform executive decision-making.
Operational Governance: Govern the successful execution of brand strategies across the organization, maintaining a healthy and high-performing brand portfolio.
Expert Consultation: Act as the primary senior operative support for Brand Managers, providing expert guidance on critical decisions that impact brand image and equity.
Corporate Brand Stewardship: Lead the governance and measurement of the company’s internal brand strategy, working strategically with PX and HR teams.
Performance Tracking: Analyse and share deep-dive insights into the long-term development of brands with Heads of Countries and other key stakeholders.
About You
The Essentials
Experience: Significant experience (ideally 5+ years) in brand communication and strategy.
Analytical Rigor: Advanced analytical skills with the ability to translate complex data (market share, demographics) into actionable brand insights.
Strategic Mindset: A high-level understanding of brand positioning within a competitive, fast-paced global market.
Technical Proficiency: Strong knowledge of Excel and data visualization tools for reporting.
Soft Skills: You are a passionate, collaborative leader with a solution-oriented mindset who excels at influencing stakeholders at all levels.
Nice to Haves
Experience in the iGaming industry or another highly regulated digital sector.
Proven track record in managing or mentoring junior strategists or cross-functional teams.
Advanced understanding of performance-led design and how it intersects with brand identity.
So what can you expect from ComeOn as a place of work?
ComeOn offers a competitive and consistent benefits package for our team members. While some benefits vary by location due to local needs and regulations, many are available company-wide, and we’re proud to offer them to everyone:
Fantastic quarterly or half-yearly team events.
Regular company events.
A 250 euro One-time Hybrid Allowance to help you set up your home office (or equivalent in local currency).
Annual wellbeing allowance/ gym membership (location dependent).
40 days ‘Work from Another Location’ (WFAL) entitlement to work from any other location around the world.
Regular Meals at the office.
At ComeOn, we have adapted a Hybrid Work model which means that we offer our employees an option to pick their workstation for the day. Want to work from the office a few days a week and some days from home? That’s totally fine with us! ComeOn is known for their inviting and vibrant offices that offer creative areas for collaboration and desk set-ups designed for a Hybrid Work model. When you visit one of our offices you will always be welcomed by a friendly face that will take good care of you. It’s our curiosity that drives our innovative business forward and we work hard in a run together spirit and we always make sure to top it up by having fun! You will not regret picking ComeOn as your next place of work.
At ComeOn Group we value diversity and we take concrete action to ensure fairness in our recruitment process. We are committed to assess candidates on their merit without discriminating against any applicant
for whatever reason that is protected by law. We truly value you, as you are.
Want to know more about us just visit our website www.comeon-group.com or any of our social media channels to take a deep dive into our culture!

Act as a primary support for Brand Managers, providing expert guidance on critical decisions impacting brand image and equity.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have significant experience in brand communication and strategy, ideally 5+ years. Advanced analytical skills and a strategic mindset are essential, along with proficiency in Excel and data visualization tools.

Operating more than 20 brands on its proprietary platform providing a secure, innovative and entertainment-led player experience across casino and sports betting. ComeOn Group is an international employer within the iGaming industry and has over 500 talented employees across 7 main locations. 

So - what can you expect from ComeOn as a place of work? 

At ComeOn, we have adapted a Hybrid Work model which means that we offer our employees an option to pick their workstation for the day. Want to work from the office a few days a week and some days from home? That’s totally fine with us! ComeOn is known for their inviting and vibrant offices that offer creative areas for collaboration and desk set-ups designed for a Hybrid Work model. When you visit one of our offices you will always be welcomed by a friendly face that will take good care of you. It’s our curiosity that drives our innovative business forward and we work hard in a #runtogether spirit and we always make sure to top it up by #havingfun! 

The ComeOn Moment

That moment when right before the roulette ball stops, or during when the penalty shoots out in the world cup final, when time slows down, when every heartbeat can be felt, when you think, whisper or scream ComeOn, ComeOn, ComeOn! 

You don’t have to be a gambler to work at ComeOn Group, but you have to understand the power of having something at stake, with the potential to win or lose. We have all experienced this, if not with money on the table, maybe it was our hearts!

title: Senior Workday Analyst
description_text:
Location: Cardiff, London, Manchester or Southampton 
Hybrid working: All Starling colleagues spend a minimum of 10 days per month in the office with flexibility to work the rest from home.
Closing date: Friday 13th February, 10am. 
About Starling 
At Starling, we are on a mission to provide people with a fairer, smarter, and more human alternative to traditional banks. We are building a leading digital bank, driven by fast technology, fair service, and honest values. Our vision is to create a banking industry that serves everyone, which we accomplish by developing best-in-class proprietary technology, offering an exceptional customer experience, and establishing a strong, sustainable business.
Our five core values – Listen, Keep It Simple, Do The Right Thing, Own It, and Aim for Greatness – are at the heart of everything we do, guiding our strategy and shaping our culture. We're a branchless, largely paperless bank, committed to responsible banking, sustainability, and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.
The Opportunity
This is a brand new role that will support the business with the implementation, embedding and ongoing road map of activities for Workday – in the main, HCM, Payroll, Absence, Performance, Benefits, Advanced Compensation and integrations.
Key Accountabilities and Responsibilities:
Leads the configuration, optimisation, and maintenance of the Workday tenant across various modules (HCM, Recruiting, Talent). Drives strategic, long-term improvements in user experience and data quality.
Acts as a subject matter expert, taking ownership of projects, advising on industry standards, and influencing business decisions through configuration. Proactively challenge existing configurations.
Expert-level configuration across modules like HCM, Time Off, Absence, and Compensation.
Leading projects from conception to deployment.
Partnering with stakeholders to define and execute business strategies.
Deep understanding of Workday methodologies and reporting.
Assist in the review, testing, implementation, training, and communication of Workday system changes and upgrades including the bi-annual Workday releases
Ability to interpret business problems into system solutions, ensuring system functionality is reflective of business and people policies
Comfortable and confident in working with large volumes of personal and sensitive data
Help identify process effectiveness and efficiency opportunities 
Provide support to other functions for HR systems/platforms, which could include Human Capital Management, Onboarding, Performance, etc.
Support on ensuring that Workday is fully integrated with key business systems
Perform detailed manipulation of complex data via tools such as spreadsheets and EIB's.
Ensuring the system is intuitive for employees and managers
Documenting system processes and access, and ensuring this is maintained accurately
What You'll Bring
At Starling, aptitude and attitude are just as crucial as formal qualifications. We're seeking a Workday expert who thrives in our fast-paced, dynamic, and technology-driven environment.
Behaviours & Competencies:
Exceptional attention to detail and ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
Effective communicator and team player with a problem-solving mindset.
The ability to work with autonomy.
Essential Skills & Experience:
Minimum of 3 years experience of Workday 
Workday configuration experience
Knowledge of Workday reporting, integrations, and data analysis tools
Knowledge of excel and EIBs
Why Join Starling?
Make an Impact: Work on projects that directly shape the future of banking and improve the financial lives of our customers. Starling is technology-led, and you'll have the freedom to accomplish your goals in innovative ways.
Culture of Excellence: Be part of a collaborative, empowered, and forward-thinking team. We encourage experimentation, ownership, and a relentless focus on "doing the right thing.”
Growth and Development: We are committed to your professional growth, offering opportunities to learn new technologies, take on new challenges, and own interesting things from day one.
A Bank That Cares: We're a Living Wage employer, committed to flexible working, and dedicated to creating a fair, open, and safe working environment with compassion and inclusion at its core.
Comprehensive Benefits: We offer a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package, including:
Company-enhanced salary sacrifice pension scheme (7% employer contribution) 
Private Medical Insurance with Vitality including mental health support and cancer care. Partner benefits include discounts with Waitrose, Mr&Mrs Smith and Peloton.
25 days holiday (plus take your public holiday allowance whenever works best for you)
An extra day’s holiday for your birthday
Life Insurance at 4x your salary.
16 hours of paid volunteering time a year.
Ability to buy or sell annual leave.
Generous family-friendly policies.
Annual leave is increased with length of service, and you can choose to buy or sell up to five extra days off
Incentivised refer a friend scheme
Perkbox membership giving access to retail discounts, a wellness platform for physical and mental health, and weekly free and boosted perks
Access to initiatives like Cycle to Work, Salary Sacrificed Gym partnerships and Electric Vehicle (EV) leasingOur Commitment to Equality
Starling Bank is an equal opportunity employer, and we’re proud of our ongoing efforts to foster diversity & inclusion in the workplace. Individuals seeking employment at Starling Bank are considered without regard to race, religion, national origin, age, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, medical condition, ancestry, physical or mental disability, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. When you provide us with this information, you are doing so at your own consent, with full knowledge that we will process this personal data in accordance with our Privacy Notice. By submitting your application, you agree that Starling Bank will collect your personal data for recruiting and related purposes. Our Privacy Notice explains what personal information we will process, where we will process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over our use of your personal information.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Workday Analyst will lead the configuration, optimization, and maintenance of the Workday tenant across various modules, driving long-term improvements in user experience and data quality. They will act as a subject matter expert, advising on industry standards and influencing business decisions through configuration.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a minimum of 3 years of Workday experience, particularly in configuration, reporting, and data analysis tools. Strong communication skills and the ability to work autonomously in a fast-paced environment are essential.

Beresford House, Town Quay, Southampton, SO14 2AQ, GB

linkedin_org_description:
Hello, we’re Starling.

Banking was broken – so we decided to fix it. The vision? Fast technology, fair service and honest values. All at the tap of a phone, all the time.

We built Britain’s first digital bank.

One hard-won banking licence later, we set about giving people a new way to spend, save and manage their money (and take better care of the planet, too).

We’re changing banking for good.

Back then, we were obsessed with unravelling the knotty world of finance and solving people’s problems rather than selling them stuff. We still are.

Since then, we’ve grown. A lot.

Over four million accounts (and four account types!). A team of thousands. Headquartered in London with offices in Cardiff, Dublin, Manchester and Southampton. Seven years voted Which? Recommended Provider and Britain's Best Banking Brand. Still zero branches.

Our culture is open, inclusive and focused on solving real customer problems, with an emphasis on doing the right thing, even when it’s not always the easy thing. From our approach to working together and sustainability to how we build our products, our decisions need to make the world – and Starling – a better place to be.

Everyone at Starling is essential to our mission, which is really quite simple: to solve our customer’s problems – and build the best bank in the world!

And now we're providing Starling to other banks, via a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) proposition through our subsidiary Engine by Starling, using the proprietary technology platform that it uses to power our own bank.

One hard-won banking licence later, we set about giving people a new way to spend, save and manage their money (and take better care of the planet, too).

We’re changing banking for good.

Back then, we were obsessed with unravelling the knotty world of finance and solving people’s problems rather than selling them stuff. We still are.

Since then, we’ve grown. A lot.

Over four million accounts (and four account types!). A team of thousands. Headquartered in London with offices in Cardiff, Dublin, Manchester and Southampton. Seven years voted Which? Recommended Provider and Britain's Best Banking Brand. Still zero branches.

Our culture is open, inclusive and focused on solving real customer problems, with an emphasis on doing the right thing, even when it’s not always the easy thing. From our approach to working together and sustainability to how we build our products, our decisions need to make the world – and Starling – a better place to be.

Everyone at Starling is essential to our mission, which is really quite simple: to solve our customer’s problems – and build the best bank in the world!

And now we're providing Starling to other banks, via a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) proposition through our subsidiary Engine by Starling, using the proprietary technology platform that it uses to power our own bank.

title: Business Manager
description_text:
About the Team

Meesho is the most downloaded e-commerce app in India. And, as the Business team, we have the most bragging rights to this claim. Why? Because we hold end-to-end responsibility to launch and scale up categories, by ensuring the right selection, competitive pricing, and conversion. And we take this job very seriously. 

As a Business Manager, you will guide our Associate Business Managers, Key Account Managers, and Business Development Executives to drive growth across all categories. You will also work closely with the Category Marketing teams and coordinate with functions such as product, supplier growth, user growth, and Fulfillment & Experience. We focus on personal growth and fun at work just as much as we do on working hard. That is why, we have regular 1-1s, monthly outings, and timely rewards and recognitions.

About the Role

As a Business Manager, you will manage both the demand and supply sides of the business. You will also ensure all the best prices and choices are showcased on the app to the customers. You will own P&L (profit and loss account) and constantly drive value to your resellers. Ultimately, you will make sure the users transacting in your portfolio keep on increasing, and also that your suppliers continue to thrive.
\n

What you will do
You will drive marketplace growth of the category, keeping in mind the consumer and the competition
You will own the P&L (profit and loss) statement for the relevant categories in alignment with business goals
You will be responsible for revenue targets as well as gross margins
You will be the face of Meesho for brands and own relationships with them 
You will be responsible for merchandising, assortment planning, and option planning pre-season and in-season
You will identify broader trends and fill in category gaps
You will coordinate with marketing, supply chain, cataloging, finance, commercial and other functions of the organization
You will drive visibility plans, and promotion plans, and coordinate between the internal marketing teams and of users

What you will need
Master's degree
At least 3+ years of experience
Strong analytical aptitude in problem-solving (basically we are looking at hustlers!)
Strong bias for speed for execution 
Ability to multitask, think critically and execute tactically
Have a proven track record of finding innovative solutions to business problems
Additional bonus if you have worked on 0-1 business or or experience in startups

\n
About us 

Welcome to Meesho, where every story begins with a spark of inspiration and a dash of entrepreneurial spirit. We're not just a platform; we're your partner in turning dreams into realities.

Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.

Our Mission

Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.

We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.

Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.

Culture and Total Rewards

Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.

Total rewards at Meesho comprise a comprehensive set of elements — monetary, non-monetary, tangible, and intangible. Our 9 guiding principles, or "Mantras," are the backbone of how we operate, influencing everything from recognition and evaluation to growth discussions. Daily rituals and processes like “Problem First Mindset,” “Listen or Die,” our Internal Mobility Program, Talent Reviews, and Continuous Performance Management embody these principles.

We offer competitive compensation — both cash and equity-based — tailored to job roles, individual experience, and skill, along with employee-centric benefits and a supportive work environment. Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.

To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.

Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/

linkedin_org_description:
Meesho is India’s e-commerce marketplace, on a mission to democratise internet commerce. Our multi-sided technology platform connects four key stakeholders — consumers, sellers, logistics partners, and content creators — to power inclusive growth at scale. 

We enable individuals and small businesses to sell online with ease, offering access to a wide customer base, integrated logistics, payment solutions, and platform support. For customers, Meesho offers a broad and affordable selection, tailored for diverse needs across Bharat. We also empower creators to build commerce-driven content that drives discovery and engagement.
Our logistics operations are powered by Valmo, Meesho’s asset-light logistics platform that works entirely through partner-led infrastructure to ensure cost-efficient and scalable deliveries.
